meta.title: ProcessIQ — O2C L4 Process Maps
meta.note: AS-IS swimlane maps generated once via the 3-step architect pipeline (Step 1 description -> Step 2 swimlane JSON) and cached here. Keyed by L4 slug; loaded in-page when an L4 cell is clicked.

order-intake-validation.name: Order Intake & Validation
order-intake-validation.description: Order Intake & Validation is triggered when a customer purchase order arrives into the order desk and exists to convert that raw demand signal into a clean, commercially sound sales order that the rest of the Order-to-Cash chain can execute against without downstream rework. Customer Service Representatives capture the incoming order and Order Management Specialists cross-reference it against the maintained customer master, ship-to hierarchy, agreed pricing conditions and product listing to confirm that every line is complete, sellable and correctly priced, drawing on master data, trade terms and availability signals as their core inputs. Where data is missing or ambiguous the order is parked and a clarification request is issued back to the customer, forming a controlled loop that only releases the order once the gap is closed, while pricing or terms that breach commercial guardrails are escalated to a Sales Operations Manager for adjudication rather than being silently accepted. The process hands a fully validated sales order forward to Order Modification and Credit Check, delivering the business value of a right-first-time order that minimises fulfilment errors, protects margin and accelerates clean revenue realisation.

sipoc.process: The Customer Service Representative keys the customer, ship-to, requested delivery date and ordered SKUs from the inbound purchase order into a Captured Sales Order Draft, transcribing line quantities and unit-of-measure against the customer's standing order profile.

The Order Management Specialist reconciles each order line against the customer master, ship-to hierarchy and active product listing, confirming the account is unblocked and every SKU is listed and sellable to that trade channel to produce a Master-Matched Order Draft.

The Customer Service Representative itemises the missing or conflicting fields — unlisted SKU, absent ship-to, unpriced line — and transmits a structured Order Clarification Request to the customer contact, flagging the order as held pending response.

The Customer supplies the corrected purchase-order details for the flagged lines, confirming substitutions, ship-to and quantities so the desk can resume validation, producing a Returned Order Clarification.

The Order Management Specialist applies the customer's contracted price list, active trade promotions and payment terms to every line, recomputing net values and comparing them to the agreed condition records to produce a Priced Sales Order.

The Order Management Specialist runs an availability-to-promise read against unrestricted stock and inbound supply for each line, assigning a confirmed delivery date and flagging any constrained SKU to produce a Confirmed Sales Order.

The Sales Operations Manager weighs the discount depth, margin floor and customer trade-investment budget against the requested price, deciding whether the deviation is commercially defensible to produce a Pricing Exception Ruling.

The Sales Operations Manager posts the sanctioned override to the order's condition record, annotates the approval rationale and margin impact, and re-confirms availability to produce a Confirmed Sales Order.

The Order Management Specialist commits the priced, available and sanctioned order to the sales order book, locking the header and lines and stamping a validation status so it is ready for change control, producing a Validated Sales Order.

data.label: Validated Sales Order - Ready for Order Changes

order-modification-cancellation.name: Order Modification / Cancellation
order-modification-cancellation.description: Order Modification / Cancellation is triggered when a customer requests a change to, or withdrawal of, an already validated sales order and exists to apply that change in a controlled way that keeps the order commercially and logistically consistent before it moves to credit and fulfilment. A Customer Service Representative logs the change request and an Order Management Specialist assesses the order's current status, placing a temporary hold and calculating the impact of the requested amendment on pricing, availability and delivery commitments using the live order record and supply signals as inputs. Where the order has already shipped the request cannot be executed cleanly and is escalated to the logistics team for manual resolution, and where the amendment exceeds the specialist's authority — a large value swing, margin erosion or a cancellation penalty — it is routed to a Sales Operations Manager whose approval or rejection governs whether the change proceeds. Approved changes are processed, the hold is released and a confirmation is issued to the customer; the process hands a modified sales order forward to Credit Check, delivering the value of accurate, auditable order changes that prevent fulfilment errors and protect revenue integrity.

sipoc.process: The Customer Service Representative records the requested amendment — quantity change, line addition, date pull-in or full cancellation — against the referenced order, capturing the reason code and requesting party to produce a Logged Modification Request.

The Order Management Specialist interrogates the order's fulfilment stage — picked, staged, invoiced or shipped — and the delivery block status to determine whether the requested change can still be applied, producing an Order Status Assessment.

The Sales Operations Manager raises a returns-or-recall exception to the logistics team, detailing the shipped quantities and carrier reference so the change can be resolved through reverse logistics, producing a Logistics Exception Ticket.
The Order Management Specialist applies a delivery block to freeze picking and shipping while the change is evaluated, timestamping the hold reason to produce an Order Hold Notice.

The Order Management Specialist recomputes order value, margin, availability and any cancellation penalty implied by the requested change, comparing the delta to the specialist authority threshold to produce a Change Impact Assessment.

The Order Management Specialist edits the order lines, quantities and dates in line with the request, re-triggering availability and pricing so the record reflects the agreed change, producing an Amended Sales Order.

The Sales Operations Manager weighs the value swing, margin impact and customer relationship against trade policy and cancellation terms to decide whether the amendment is authorised, producing a Modification Review Decision.

The Sales Operations Manager posts the sanctioned amendment to the order lines and dates, annotating the approval rationale and any penalty waiver so the record carries a full audit trail, producing an Amended Sales Order.
The Order Management Specialist removes the delivery block once the change is committed and re-validates that the order is consistent and executable, producing a Released Sales Order.

The Customer Service Representative compiles the revised order terms, quantities and delivery dates into a customer-facing confirmation and dispatches it, producing a Modified Sales Order - Ready for Credit Check.
sipoc.output: Modified Sales Order - Ready for Credit Check

data.label: Modified Sales Order - Ready for Credit Check

credit-check-approval.name: Credit Check & Approval
credit-check-approval.description: Credit Check & Approval is triggered when a modified sales order is presented for financial clearance and exists to protect the business from over-exposure by confirming that the customer has the credit standing to absorb the order before any stock is committed. An Order Management Specialist compiles the customer's live exposure — open receivables, unbilled orders and the assigned credit limit — and a Credit Risk Analyst scores creditworthiness against risk category, payment history and available headroom, using the credit master and ageing position as core inputs. Orders that sit within limit are cleared straight through, while orders that breach the limit are held and escalated to a Credit Manager who adjudicates whether to grant a temporary increase, require prepayment or block the order, ensuring exceptions are decided by the right authority rather than defaulting to release. The process hands a credit-approved sales order forward to Outbound Delivery, delivering the value of controlled credit exposure, reduced bad-debt risk and a clean, fundable order ready to fulfil.

sipoc.process: The Order Management Specialist assembles the customer's open receivables, unbilled order backlog and assigned credit limit against the value of the presented order to produce a Customer Credit Exposure File.

The Credit Risk Analyst evaluates the customer's risk category, days-beyond-terms history and remaining credit headroom against the order value, computing the projected exposure to produce a Credit Risk Scorecard.
The Credit Risk Analyst removes the automatic credit block and stamps the order as within sanctioned exposure, recording the headroom consumed to produce a Credit-Cleared Order Record.
The Credit Risk Analyst quantifies the amount by which the order exceeds available headroom and documents the exposure drivers and ageing position to produce a Credit Hold Notice.

The Credit Manager weighs the customer's strategic value, payment trajectory and security position against the exposure breach to decide whether to extend credit, require prepayment or block, producing a Credit Hold Ruling.
The Credit Manager posts a temporary limit uplift with an expiry and rationale, releasing the credit block within the newly sanctioned headroom to produce a Credit-Cleared Order Record.
The Order Management Specialist confirms the credit block is lifted and the exposure ledger updated, releasing the order to fulfilment scheduling to produce a Credit-Approved Sales Order.

data.label: Credit-Approved Sales Order - Ready for Fulfilment

outbound-delivery-fulfilment.name: Outbound Delivery & Fulfilment
outbound-delivery-fulfilment.description: Outbound Delivery & Fulfilment is triggered when a credit-approved sales order is ready to ship and exists to convert that authorised demand into a physical, dispatch-ready delivery while honouring the promised service level. An Order Fulfilment Coordinator generates the outbound delivery and a Warehouse Operations Lead reserves stock and assembles the pick list, using live inventory, batch and warehouse-capacity signals as inputs to confirm the order can be served complete. Where stock falls short the shortfall is logged and escalated to a Logistics Dispatch Manager who decides whether a partial shipment is acceptable or the delivery must wait for replenishment, ensuring service and cost trade-offs are made by the right authority rather than defaulting to a split. Confirmed picks are packed, staged and released for carrier collection; the process hands a released outbound delivery forward to Stock Allocation and downstream logistics, delivering the value of on-time, in-full fulfilment that protects customer service and unlocks billing.

sipoc.process: The Order Fulfilment Coordinator creates the outbound delivery document from the approved order, grouping lines by ship-to and route and assigning the delivering plant to produce an Outbound Delivery Note.
The Warehouse Operations Lead allocates unrestricted stock to each delivery line by batch and storage bin, checking shelf-life and quality status to produce a Stock Reservation Record.
The Warehouse Operations Lead sequences the reserved lines into an optimised pick walk by zone and generates the picking instructions to produce a Delivery Pick List.
The Order Fulfilment Coordinator records the shorted lines and quantities, notes the next replenishment ETA and the customer's service tier to produce a Stock Shortfall Notice.

The Logistics Dispatch Manager weighs the customer's on-time-in-full agreement, extra freight cost of a split and replenishment ETA to decide whether to ship partial or hold, producing a Partial Shipment Ruling.
The Logistics Dispatch Manager splits the delivery to the available quantities, schedules the backorder line and records the service-level exception to produce a Delivery Pick List.
The Warehouse Operations Lead confirms picked quantities, packs to handling-unit standard and stages the load at the assigned dock door to produce a Staged Shipment Unit.
The Logistics Dispatch Manager confirms goods-issue against the staged load and tenders it to the assigned carrier, locking the delivery for transport to produce a Released Outbound Delivery.

data.label: Released Outbound Delivery - Ready for Stock Allocation

data-prep-request-validation.name: Data Prep & Request Validation
data-prep-request-validation.description: Triggered whenever a Sales, Customer Service, or Procurement business user raises a new or change request for a customer or material master record, this process exists to intake the request, confirm it carries every mandatory field required by the customer or material template, and screen it for duplication before any collection effort begins; the Master Data Requestor submits the request form citing business justification and effective date, the Master Data Steward logs it into the intake queue with an SLA priority tier and cross-references it against existing records using tax ID/DUNS or GTIN/part-number matching augmented by fuzzy name search, and the Data Governance Analyst classifies its sensitivity tier based on customer credit exposure or material regulatory/allergen classification; when mandatory fields are missing the Steward dispatches a clarification notice and awaits a resubmission within a defined SLA, escalating to suspension if the requestor remains non-responsive past the follow-up threshold, while a confirmed duplicate is rejected outright with no new record created; the Master Data Manager then packages the validated, uniquely-screened, sensitivity-tiered request for handoff, producing a Validated Master Data Request Package that is ready for the Data Collection & Validation team to begin populating the record.

sipoc.process: captures the request type (new or change), business justification, requested effective date, and the mandatory field set defined by the customer or material data template
logs the request into the intake queue, assigns a unique request ID and SLA priority tier, and checks the submission channel and attachment completeness
searches the master data repository by tax ID/DUNS number for customer requests or GTIN/manufacturer part number for material requests, combined with a fuzzy name-and-address match
identifies which mandatory fields are missing against the customer or material template and drafts a clarification notice citing the specific field gaps and a resubmission deadline
monitors the response queue against the 3-business-day SLA window for the requestor to supply the missing mandatory fields

data.label: Duplicate Request Rejected — No New Record Created

sipoc.process: assigns a governance sensitivity tier based on the customer's credit exposure band or the material's regulatory/allergen classification, which determines the downstream approval routing
compiles the validated request form, duplicate screening evidence, and sensitivity tier into a single handoff package, confirming template version alignment for the data collection team

data.label: Validated Master Data Request Package — Ready for Data Collection

data-collection-validation.name: Data Collection & Validation
data-collection-validation.description: Beginning the moment the Master Data Manager hands off a Validated Master Data Request Package, this process exists to pull the actual attribute values from source documentation and systems and assemble them into a clean, cross-system-consistent draft master record before it can be sent forward for approval; the Master Data Steward retrieves source attributes from documentation such as a Dun & Bradstreet report, certificate of insurance, or material specification sheet and maps them into the template using standard value lists for units of measure, payment terms, and tax classification, after which the Data Governance Analyst scrutinizes the draft against automated data-quality rules covering field format, cross-field consistency, and reference-table validity; when mandatory field values are still missing the Steward dispatches a data gap notice to the requestor or source-system owner and awaits an update within SLA, suspending the ticket if the follow-up threshold is exceeded, while any data-quality exceptions found during the scan are corrected by the Governance Analyst and the record is rescanned until clean; once clean, the Steward reconciles pricing group, credit terms, and plant/distribution channel assignments against the ERP, CRM, and finance systems of record, and the Master Data Manager compiles the reconciled record with its quality and reconciliation evidence into a Data-Validated Master Record Package ready for governance approval.

data.label: Validated Master Data Request Package — Ready for Data Collection

sipoc.input: Validated Master Data Request Package — Ready for Data Collection
sipoc.process: pulls the required field set from source systems and requestor-supplied documentation, such as a Dun & Bradstreet report, certificate of insurance, or material specification sheet, per the request's template version
maps each source attribute into the corresponding customer or material master field, applying standard value lists such as unit-of-measure codes, payment terms codes, and tax classification codes
runs the record through automated data-quality rules checking field format, cross-field consistency such as country code versus tax ID format, and reference-table validity
flags the specific blank or invalid mandatory fields against the template and routes a data gap notice to the original requestor or source-system owner with a 2-business-day resubmission deadline
monitors the data gap queue against the 2-business-day SLA for the requestor or source-system owner to supply the updated field values

reviews each flagged exception against the source documentation and corrects the field value directly, or requests a source data correction from the Master Data Steward when the source itself is invalid

cross-checks the draft record's key fields, including pricing group, credit terms, and plant/distribution channel assignment, against the ERP, CRM, and finance systems of record to confirm no conflicting values exist
assembles the reconciled record together with the data quality scan results and reconciliation evidence into a single package for governance approval

data.label: Data-Validated Master Record Package — Ready for Approval

approval-syndication.name: Approval & Syndication
approval-syndication.description: Starting from the Data-Validated Master Record Package handed off by the collection team, this process exists to obtain the required governance and financial sign-offs and then publish and distribute the golden record so every downstream system operates from the same authoritative version; the Master Data Manager routes the package according to its sensitivity tier while the Data Governance Analyst scrutinizes it against segregation-of-duties rules, regulatory labeling or allergen-declaration completeness for materials, and trade-compliance or sanctions screening for new customers, after which the Finance Controller endorses the credit limit or standard cost/list price fields against the delegation-of-authority matrix; a compliance failure is returned to the Master Data Steward with correction instructions and rechecked within SLA, with the ticket suspended if the requestor's correction remains non-responsive past the follow-up threshold, while a denied finance endorsement ends the request outright as a permanent rejection; once both sign-offs are secured, the Master Data Manager commits the record as the golden master with its permanent master number and effective date, then syndicates it to the ERP, CRM, pricing, and logistics systems with receipt acknowledgment confirmed from every subscriber, producing a Syndicated Master Record that is ready to enter ongoing maintenance.

data.label: Data-Validated Master Record Package — Ready for Approval

sipoc.input: Data-Validated Master Record Package — Ready for Approval
sipoc.process: determines the approval routing path from the sensitivity tier assigned during collection, directing customer credit-bearing records toward Finance Controller sign-off and regulated material attributes toward Data Governance Analyst sign-off
checks the record against segregation-of-duties rules, regulatory labeling or allergen declaration completeness for materials, and trade-compliance/sanctions screening for new customers
reviews the credit limit assignment or standard cost/list price fields for financial policy alignment and confirms no unauthorized override of the delegation-of-authority approval matrix

identifies the specific compliance failure, such as a missing allergen declaration, incomplete sanctions screening, or an unauthorized credit tier assignment, and returns the package with correction instructions and a 2-business-day deadline
monitors the correction queue against the 2-business-day SLA for the steward to resubmit the corrected record for a compliance recheck
commits the approved record as the golden master in the system of record, assigning the permanent customer or material master number and effective date

data.label: Master Record Rejected — Finance Endorsement Denied

sipoc.process: distributes the golden record to the ERP, CRM, pricing, and logistics systems per the syndication distribution list and confirms a receipt acknowledgment from each subscribing system

data.label: Syndicated Master Record — Ready for Ongoing Maintenance

ongoing-data-maintenance.name: Ongoing Data Maintenance
ongoing-data-maintenance.description: Once a record is syndicated and live, this process exists to keep the customer or material master accurate and current over its operating life by capturing every subsequent change trigger, whether a requestor-submitted update, a periodic revalidation due date, or a data-decay alert such as a failed delivery or an expired certification, and carrying it through to a re-syndicated update; the Master Data Steward logs the maintenance ticket and, once its fields are complete, applies the change to a working copy of the record while preserving the currently syndicated version as a fallback, after which the Data Governance Analyst assesses the change against governance thresholds such as credit-limit increase percentage, material cost variance, or regulatory reclassification; an incomplete change request triggers a clarification cycle with the requestor that suspends the ticket if the follow-up threshold is breached, while a change that exceeds the approval threshold is escalated out of this process and routed back into Approval & Syndication for re-endorsement rather than committed directly; changes within threshold are committed in place by the Master Data Manager, incrementing the record's change history and effective-dated field version, then re-syndicated to the ERP, CRM, pricing, and logistics systems with acknowledgment confirmed from each subscriber, leaving a Maintained Customer Master Record ready for Order Intake & Validation to consume.

data.label: Syndicated Master Record — Ready for Ongoing Maintenance

sipoc.input: Syndicated Master Record — Ready for Ongoing Maintenance
sipoc.process: captures the maintenance trigger, whether a requestor-submitted change request, a periodic revalidation due date, or a data-decay alert such as a failed delivery or expired certification, logs it against the existing master record, and assigns a maintenance ticket ID
updates the affected fields on a working copy of the record, such as ship-to address, credit limit, or material standard cost, while preserving the currently syndicated version as the fallback
identifies the missing or invalid fields on the change request against the maintenance template and drafts a clarification notice citing the specific gaps and a 2-business-day resubmission deadline

assesses the change against governance thresholds, including credit limit increase percentage, material cost variance, or regulatory reclassification, to determine whether it requires re-approval before it can be committed
monitors the response queue against the 2-business-day SLA for the requestor to supply the missing change details

data.label: Change Escalated — Routed to Approval & Syndication for Re-Endorsement

sipoc.process: updates the golden master record in place, incrementing the record's change history log and effective-dated field version
propagates the field-level delta to the ERP, CRM, pricing, and logistics systems and confirms a receipt acknowledgment from each subscribing system

data.label: Maintained Customer Master Record — Ready for Order Intake

stock-allocation-mgmt.name: Stock Allocation & Management
stock-allocation-mgmt.description: Triggered when an order is released from Outbound Delivery & Fulfilment as a Released Outbound Delivery, this process exists so the Order Fulfilment Coordinator, Inventory Planner, Warehouse Operations Lead, and Distribution Allocation Analyst can convert an approved order into a firm, physically-backed stock reservation before a delivery document is created; the coordinator validates plant, SKU, quantity, and ship-to data on each order line, the inventory planner queries available-to-promise stock by batch and expiry to allocate quantities under first-expiry-first-out rules, and where full coverage is unavailable the planner requests a priority replenishment transfer from a source plant while the distribution allocation analyst monitors the transfer against the required-by date, escalating repeated delays to a held status pending replenishment; once stock is available in full, the warehouse operations lead confirms the specific bin and lot assignment against the system reservation, the coordinator reconciles any variance between reserved and physically counted stock, and the process closes when the coordinator finalizes and issues a Stock Allocation Confirmation that hands the locked reservation to Delivery Creation for delivery-document generation.

sipoc.process: Cross-check plant, SKU, requested quantity, ship-to address, and requested delivery date on each order line against the released delivery header
Query on-hand and incoming stock by plant, SKU, and batch/expiry against requested quantity and requested delivery date to determine coverage
Reserve stock quantities against each order line by batch/lot under first-expiry-first-out sequencing rules
Submit a stock transfer request to the nearest source plant or distribution center citing the shortage quantity, SKU, and required-by date
Verify the physical bin location, lot/batch number, and expiry date align with the system reservation and FEFO policy
Monitor the transfer order status and source-plant dispatch schedule against the required-by date
Cross-check the reserved system quantity against the confirmed physical bin count and resolve any variance before handoff
Lock the allocation in the system, timestamp the reservation, and prepare the handoff package for delivery-document creation

delivery-creation.name: Delivery Creation
delivery-creation.description: Beginning when the Order Fulfilment Coordinator receives the Stock Allocation Confirmation from Stock Allocation & Management, this process exists to turn a confirmed stock reservation into a shipment-ready delivery document set for the Order Fulfilment Coordinator, Warehouse Operations Lead, Transportation Planner, and Documentation Specialist; the coordinator first determines whether the order must split across multiple deliveries based on ship-to address, requested date, and carrier capacity, the warehouse operations lead creates the delivery document(s) referencing allocated batches and quantities and checks the document for consistency, with missing weight, dimension, or batch data flagged back to the coordinator for correction and re-checked, delays past the resolution window putting the delivery on documentation hold; the warehouse operations lead then assigns a shipping point and default route, the transportation planner validates contracted carrier capacity and transit lane for that route and date, the documentation specialist produces the packing list, bill-of-lading draft, and any regulated-product compliance certificates required for the destination market, and the process closes when the coordinator confirms the delivery is complete and hands off a Confirmed Delivery For Dispatch package to Logistics Operations for carrier tendering and load planning.

sipoc.process: Interrogate ship-to address, requested delivery date, route schedule, and carrier capacity constraints to decide whether the order splits into multiple deliveries
Generate delivery document(s) in the system referencing allocated batches, quantities, and ship-to per split and assign delivery numbers
Determine the shipping point/loading dock and default route schedule per ship-to zone and carrier lane agreement
Identify missing or mismatched fields such as batch assignment, net weight, or dimensions and route a correction request to the originating master-data or allocation source
Check contracted carrier capacity, transit lane, and service level for the assigned route and delivery date
Track resolution of the flagged data issue against the SLA clock and confirm the updated document reflects corrected batch/weight data
Produce the packing list, bill-of-lading draft, and any regulated-product compliance documents (batch/expiry certificates, hazmat declarations) required for the ship-to market

data.label: Confirm Delivery Ready For Dispatch Planning

sipoc.process: Verify all delivery lines, documents, and route assignment are complete and release the delivery for carrier tendering

data.label: Confirmed Delivery For Dispatch Handed Off

logistics-operations.name: Logistics Operations
logistics-operations.description: Starting from a Confirmed Delivery For Dispatch handed off by Delivery Creation, this process exists so the Transportation Planner, Logistics Dispatch Manager, Carrier Coordinator, and Warehouse Operations Lead can turn individual confirmed deliveries into a tendered, carrier-committed transportation plan; the transportation planner consolidates deliveries by destination zone, route, and date against truck weight, volume, and pallet-position capacity into a draft load plan, the logistics dispatch manager sequences the multi-stop route by delivery window and site access constraints, and the carrier coordinator tenders the load to the contracted carrier under the applicable lane rate agreement, re-tendering to a backup carrier or spot broker when a tender is rejected and escalating to a held status if repeated tender attempts fail to secure capacity within the pickup window; once a carrier accepts, the carrier coordinator confirms a firm pickup appointment with dock door and time window, the logistics dispatch manager issues the approved transportation plan against the shipping calendar, and the process closes when the warehouse operations lead schedules the dock door and load sequence and releases the Dock Load Schedule to Warehouse Management & Logistics for physical picking and loading.

sipoc.process: Group deliveries by destination zone, route, and delivery date against truck weight, volume, and pallet-position capacity to build optimal load plans
Sequence multi-stop routes by delivery window, distance, and site access constraints
Submit the load tender to the contracted carrier per the lane agreement and rate card, specifying pickup window and equipment type
Schedule a firm pickup appointment with the accepted carrier confirming dock door, time window, and driver check-in requirements
Resubmit the load to the next-ranked carrier in the routing guide or a spot-market broker within the pickup-window tolerance
Compile the final route, load, and carrier appointment details into the transportation plan and release it against the shipping calendar
Monitor the carrier response queue against the required pickup window and escalate if unacknowledged
Reserve the dock door/time slot and align the warehouse load sequence with the confirmed carrier appointment and route stop order

data.label: Dock Load Schedule Released For Picking
Delivery Held — No Carrier Capacity Confirmed

warehouse-mgmt-logistics.name: Warehouse Management & Logistics
warehouse-mgmt-logistics.description: Opening on the Dock Load Schedule released by Logistics Operations, this process exists to physically pick, pack, load, and ship the confirmed order for the Warehouse Operations Lead, Pick-Pack Supervisor, Dock Loading Supervisor, and Carrier Coordinator; the warehouse operations lead releases a pick wave sequenced by bin location and batch expiry priority, the pick-pack supervisor directs pickers to retrieve and stage the specified quantities, and where picked quantities do not match the delivery document (short pick or batch substitution) the supervisor flags the discrepancy for bin replenishment or a documented override, with unresolved shortages past the shipping cutoff placing the delivery on hold; once quantities match, the pick-pack supervisor packs and palletizes the shipment with shipping labels, the dock loading supervisor loads the trailer at the assigned dock verifying pallet count and weight distribution, then reconciles the loaded quantities against the bill of lading and packing list, the carrier coordinator obtains the driver's signature on the bill of lading and seals the trailer, and the process closes when the warehouse operations lead posts the goods issue in the system, relieving inventory and generating the Goods Issue Confirmation that triggers Invoice Generation.

sipoc.process: Batch delivery lines into a pick wave sequenced by bin location, batch FEFO priority, and dock schedule timing
Direct pickers to retrieve specified batch/lot quantities from bin locations and stage them to the outbound marshalling lane
Pack cartons to the shipping-unit standard, build and shrink-wrap pallets, and apply shipping labels per the delivery document
Identify the short-pick or batch substitution against the delivery document and request bin replenishment or a documented supervisor override
Load pallets onto the trailer per the assigned dock door and load sequence, verifying pallet count and axle-weight distribution
Track the bin replenishment transfer or obtain a documented substitution override against the delivery cutoff time
Cross-check the loaded pallet count, weight, and seal number against the bill of lading and packing list

data.label: Obtain Driver Signature And Seal Shipment

sipoc.process: Present the bill of lading for driver signature, affix a tamper-evident trailer seal, and record the seal number
Post the goods-issue transaction in the system, relieving inventory and confirming shipment departure against the delivery document

data.label: Goods Issue Posted — Proof Of Shipment Ready

invoice-generation.name: Invoice Generation
invoice-generation.description: In a CPG billing organization, once the warehouse confirms goods issue and captures proof of delivery, the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst consolidates the delivery data against the sales order's negotiated pricing, trade-spend conditions, and tax jurisdiction codes before the Billing Specialist runs the SD billing document type (F2) in the invoicing run to generate a draft customer invoice; the Billing Team Lead validates that unit pricing and tax lines sit within contract tolerance, kicking out-of-tolerance lines back to the Billing Specialist for correction against the pricing condition master, before endorsing release against the credit-management block and delegation-of-authority matrix, after which the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst posts the billing document to FI/AR and the Billing Specialist dispatches the invoice via the customer's contracted channel (EDI 810, retailer portal, or PDF email); unacknowledged transmissions are held through the gateway's automated retry window and, if repeated retries lapse the threshold, escalated to manual PDF delivery so every invoice ultimately reaches the customer in a form ready for downstream validation.

data.label: Goods-Issued Delivery / Proof of Delivery Received

sipoc.input: Goods-Issued Delivery / Proof of Delivery Received
sipoc.process: reconcile shipped quantities, confirmed sales-order line items, and contract pricing/tax conditions from the delivery note against the customer master
run the billing document type (F2) in the invoicing run, applying the pricing procedure, tax jurisdiction code, and rebate/trade-spend conditions
confirm the billing block release, verify credit-limit exposure, and authorize the invoice for posting per the delegation-of-authority matrix
trace the flagged price or tax line back to the contract pricing condition record and correct the condition type or manually override the rate
post the billing document to FI/AR, generating the accounting document and updating the customer's open-item ledger
transmit the invoice via the customer's contracted channel (EDI 810, retailer portal upload, or email PDF) per the output preference on the customer master

data.label: Acknowledged Customer Invoice Ready for Validation

sipoc.process: hold the transmission in pending status through the EDI/portal gateway's automated retry cycle of up to three attempts over twenty-four hours
flag the account for manual invoice delivery, package a PDF copy, and dispatch it directly to the customer's AP contact with a delivery-confirmation request

data.label: Manually Delivered Customer Invoice Ready for Validation

invoice-validation-dispute-handling.name: Invoice Validation & Dispute Handling
invoice-validation-dispute-handling.description: Once the customer invoice has been delivered, the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst runs a three-way match of invoice lines against the sales order and goods-issued delivery note for quantity, price, and tax consistency; invoices with no exceptions are certified accurate by the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst and closed out by the Billing Team Lead, while any match exception is logged by the Dispute Resolution Analyst as a formal dispute case categorized by reason code (price, quantity, tax, or freight), with the Billing Specialist soliciting proof-of-delivery, signed pricing agreements, or contract addenda from the customer or sales organization; unanswered evidence requests are held in a pending-evidence queue against the contractual response window and, once that window lapses without a customer reply, the case is closed non-responsive in favor of the original invoice, whereas evidence that is received is adjudicated by the Dispute Resolution Analyst against contract terms and delivery records, and the Billing Team Lead either authorizes referral of a substantiated exception into credit/debit note processing or notifies the customer that the dispute is rejected and the original invoice stands.

data.label: Customer Invoice Delivered — Ready for Validation

sipoc.input: Customer Invoice Delivered — Ready for Validation
sipoc.process: three-way match the invoice line items against the sales order and goods-issued delivery note for quantity, unit price, and tax-code consistency
open a dispute case record capturing the exception category (price, quantity, tax, or freight) and assign a reason code per the dispute taxonomy
certify that quantities, unit prices, tax jurisdiction, and payment terms on the invoice reconcile fully to the order and delivery records
solicit proof-of-delivery, signed pricing agreements, or contract addenda from the customer or the sales/logistics function to substantiate or refute the exception
record the validation outcome in the billing exception log and release the invoice from validation-hold status
evaluate the submitted evidence against contract pricing terms and delivery records to determine whether the exception is substantiated, partially substantiated, or rejected
hold the dispute case in pending-evidence status and track elapsed days against the contractual ten-business-day response window
approve referral of the substantiated exception amount and reason code to billing adjustment processing per the delegation-of-authority threshold
close the dispute case per policy default, upholding the original invoice, once the customer response window lapses without submission
communicate the rejection rationale to the customer's AP contact and reaffirm the original invoice amount as due

credit-note-debit-note-processing.name: Credit Note / Debit Note Processing
credit-note-debit-note-processing.description: When the Billing Team Lead has authorized referral of a substantiated dispute, the Billing Specialist classifies whether the adjudicated variance direction calls for a credit note (customer overbilled) or a debit note (customer underbilled), drafting the note against the original invoice reference, the substantiated amount, and applicable tax treatment in the statutory note format; the Revenue Controller reviews credit notes against the delegation-of-authority threshold, approving those within authority directly and escalating larger amounts to the Billing Team Lead for secondary sign-off, while debit notes are approved by the Revenue Controller against the substantiated dispute case, and in both cases the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst posts the approved note to FI/AR against the original invoice, updating the customer's open-item balance and leaving it pending final adjustment reconciliation.

sipoc.process: determine whether the substantiated exception calls for a credit note (customer overbilled) or a debit note (customer underbilled) based on the adjudicated variance direction and reason code
prepare the credit note referencing the original invoice number, the substantiated variance amount, and the tax adjustment per statutory credit-note format
prepare the debit note referencing the original invoice number, the underbilled variance amount, and the tax adjustment per statutory debit-note format
validate the debit note variance amount and tax treatment against the substantiated dispute case and approve it for posting
validate the credit note amount, tax treatment, and supporting dispute-case reference against the delegation-of-authority matrix and approve it for posting
post the debit note to FI/AR against the original invoice reference, adding the underbilled amount and updating the customer's open-item balance
sipoc.output: Posted Debit Note — Pending Adjustment Reconciliation

data.label: Escalate Credit Note for Senior Approval

sipoc.process: route the credit note above the standard approval threshold to the billing team lead for secondary sign-off per the finance approval matrix
post the credit note to FI/AR against the original invoice reference, reversing the disputed amount and updating the customer's open-item balance
sipoc.output: Posted Credit Note — Pending Adjustment Reconciliation

data.label: Posted Debit Note — Pending Adjustment Reconciliation
Posted Credit Note — Pending Adjustment Reconciliation

billing-adjustments.name: Billing Adjustments
billing-adjustments.description: With the credit or debit note posted against the original invoice, the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst reconciles the note amount against the invoice's AR open-item balance; where no further adjustment is needed the Billing Specialist finalizes the balance directly, but where a residual variance remains the Billing Specialist classifies it as a routine correction (rebate, freight allowance, tax correction, or rounding tolerance) or a bad-debt write-off candidate, with the Revenue Controller posting routine corrections directly within delegated thresholds and the Invoice-to-Cash Analyst confirming the posting reconciles to the classification; write-off candidates require the Revenue Controller to solicit documented justification, such as a collections-exhaustion memo, bankruptcy notice, or dispute-settlement record, holding the case in pending-documentation status against a fifteen-business-day window, with the Billing Team Lead either approving the write-off for posting once justification arrives or, if the window lapses unsupported, rejecting the write-off and reinstating the full balance, so that every path resolves the invoice into a definitive AR open-item status ready to enter the collections strategy queue.

data.label: Posted Credit/Debit Note — Pending Adjustment Reconciliation

sipoc.input: Posted Credit/Debit Note — Pending Adjustment Reconciliation
sipoc.process: match the posted credit or debit note amount against the original invoice's AR open-item balance to confirm the net adjusted balance and residual due amount
classify the residual variance as a pricing rebate, freight allowance, tax correction, rounding tolerance, or bad-debt write-off candidate per the adjustment reason-code taxonomy
close the adjustment case and lock the invoice's AR open-item balance as final, releasing it from billing-adjustment hold
sipoc.output: Finalized Customer Invoice — AR Open Item Ready for Collections

sipoc.process: solicit documented justification, such as a collections-exhaustion memo, bankruptcy notice, or dispute-settlement record, from the collections or legal function to support the proposed write-off
apply the rebate, freight, tax, or rounding correction directly to the invoice's open-item record within the delegated posting threshold
verify the posted adjustment reflects correctly in the customer's AR open-item ledger and reconciles to the adjustment category classification
sipoc.output: Finalized Customer Invoice — AR Open Item Ready for Collections

sipoc.process: authorize the write-off amount against the delegation-of-authority matrix and instruct posting of the bad-debt adjustment to the customer's open item

data.label: Finalized Customer Invoice — AR Open Item Ready for Collections

sipoc.process: hold the write-off case in pending-documentation status and track elapsed days against the internal fifteen-business-day justification window
post the approved write-off amount to the customer's AR sub-ledger, zeroing or reducing the open-item balance and archiving the adjustment case file
sipoc.output: Write-Off Closed — Balance Removed from Collections Queue

data.label: Write-Off Closed — Balance Removed from Collections Queue

sipoc.process: decline the unsupported write-off, reinstate the full residual balance on the customer's AR open item, and close the adjustment case per policy default
sipoc.output: Write-Off Rejected — Reinstated Balance Ready for Collections

data.label: Write-Off Rejected — Reinstated Balance Ready for Collections

collections-strategy.name: Collections Strategy
collections-strategy.description: In CPG accounts receivable, the Collections Strategy process converts the finalised, dispute-cleared AR open-item position into an actioned cadence: the AR Controller extracts ageing-bucketed open items, the Collections Analyst segments accounts by DSO, credit-limit utilisation, dispute flags and promise-to-pay reliability into risk tiers, routes accounts still carrying an active dispute flag onto a hold segment outside the cadence, and prioritises the remaining high-value delinquencies for the Collections Strategy Lead, who designs bespoke engagement for named strategic customers or assigns a standard dunning cadence template from the playbook for the long tail, both routed to the Credit & Collections Manager for risk-appetite and delegation-of-authority endorsement before the Collections Analyst publishes the endorsed parameters as a live, segmented collections worklist.

data.label: Finalised Customer Invoice / AR Open Item

sipoc.input: Finalised Customer Invoice / AR Open Item
sipoc.process: AR Controller consolidates all open invoices and unapplied credit memos into ageing buckets (current, 1-30, 31-60, 61-90, 90+ days) drawn from the finalised, dispute-cleared invoice position
Analyst scores each account against DSO, credit-limit utilisation, dispute flags and historical promise-to-pay reliability to bucket accounts into Low, Medium and High risk tiers
Analyst excludes accounts carrying an unresolved billing dispute from the dunning cadence and logs them to a dispute-hold segment pending resolution
Analyst ranks the remaining accounts within each risk tier by outstanding balance and ageing bucket to surface the top-dollar delinquencies for strategy design

data.label: Dispute Hold Segment Record, Excluded from Dunning Cadence

sipoc.process: Strategy Lead tailors contact sequencing, negotiation latitude and executive escalation triggers for named strategic accounts based on commercial relationship value
Strategy Lead maps each risk tier to a pre-approved cadence template covering contact frequency, channel and tone escalation drawn from the collections playbook
Manager reviews the proposed cadence or bespoke plan against risk appetite, delegation-of-authority limits and dunning-conduct constraints before sign-off
Analyst loads the endorsed strategy parameters into the collections work-queue, assigning each account a cadence start date, owner and channel sequence

data.label: Segmented Collections Worklist, Ready for Dunning

collections-correspondence-escalation.name: Collections Correspondence & Escalation
collections-correspondence-escalation.description: Collections Correspondence & Escalation operationalises the endorsed cadence in daily CPG collections practice: the Collections Analyst releases the segmented worklist into specialist queues, the Collections Specialist dispatches the first-stage dunning notice and holds the account open across the cadence-defined response window, escalating tone and channel through successive notices while the response window has not lapsed and the account has not hit its escalation-stage threshold, and referring genuinely non-responsive debtors to the Credit & Collections Manager for formal escalation once the threshold is reached; where the customer does respond, the Collections Strategy Lead screens the reply to separate a payment-commitment signal, packaged as a negotiation-ready account record, from a dispute or general inquiry, which is instead referred back into the billing dispute queue outside the collections cadence.

data.label: Segmented Collections Worklist, Ready for Dunning

sipoc.input: Segmented Collections Worklist, Ready for Dunning
sipoc.process: Analyst distributes worklist entries to collections specialists by cadence start date, channel assignment and territory ownership
Specialist issues the first-stage reminder by email or customer portal per cadence template, referencing invoice number, due date and outstanding amount
Specialist holds the account open for the cadence-defined response window (typically 7 calendar days), watching for inbound payment, dispute or contact activity
Specialist captures the nature of the response, distinguishing a payment commitment from a dispute or general inquiry, for routing to strategy screening
Manager receives the full contact log and ageing position for accounts that have exhausted the cadence without any customer response

sipoc.input: Response Window Elapsed Record [Threshold Not Reached]
sipoc.process: Specialist escalates tone and channel, for example portal notice to phone call, per the next cadence stage and re-opens the response window

sipoc.input: Customer Response Outcome Record [Payment Commitment Indicated]
sipoc.process: Strategy Lead screens the response against negotiation eligibility, confirming no active legal dispute and no existing write-off review, before release

data.label: Formal Escalation Case Record, Non-Responsive Debtor

sipoc.input: Customer Response Outcome Record [Dispute or Inquiry Raised]
sipoc.process: Strategy Lead removes the account from the dunning cadence and packages the customer's dispute or inquiry for the billing team outside collections

data.label: Negotiation-Ready Account Record, Ready for Promise-to-Pay Capture
Dispute Referral Record, Removed from Collections Cadence

promise-to-pay-payment-plan-capture.name: Promise to Pay / Payment Plan Capture
promise-to-pay-payment-plan-capture.description: Promise to Pay / Payment Plan Capture is where the CPG collections function converts a negotiation-ready, responsive account into a documented, monitored commitment: the Collections Specialist negotiates date, amount and instalment structure against standard delegation-of-authority thresholds, capturing the commitment directly when within authority or routing to the Credit & Collections Manager for approval when the plan exceeds standard limits, with manager-rejected plans referred straight into write-off assessment; the Collections Analyst registers every confirmed commitment in the payment-due calendar, suspending active dunning, and reconciles the remittance feed against the committed due date at grace-period expiry, with the AR Controller closing honoured promises as settled while the Analyst flags commitments that miss the grace-period deadline as broken and forwards them for provisioning and write-off review.

data.label: Negotiation-Ready Account Record, Ready for Promise-to-Pay Capture

sipoc.input: Negotiation-Ready Account Record, Ready for Promise-to-Pay Capture
sipoc.process: Specialist negotiates commitment date, amount and instalment count against standard delegation-of-authority thresholds, for example up to 3 instalments within a 60-day horizon
Specialist formalises the agreed date, amount and instalment structure into the system-of-record commitment without further approval
Specialist packages the proposed terms with the account's risk profile and forwards it for delegation-of-authority approval
Manager authorises the extended instalment structure, recording approval rationale and any collateral conditions attached
Analyst closes the negotiation attempt and packages the ageing history, dispute history and rejected terms as a write-off assessment referral
Analyst logs the commitment date, amount and instalment schedule into the payment-due calendar and suppresses further dunning contact for the standby period

data.label: Rejected Payment Plan Record, Referred for Write-off Assessment

sipoc.process: Analyst reconciles the bank and ERP remittance feed against the committed due date at due-date-plus-two-days grace
Controller matches the posted remittance to the commitment record and closes the case as honoured

sipoc.input: Commitment Due-Date Monitoring Outcome [Grace Period Exhausted]
sipoc.process: Analyst flags the commitment as broken, compiling ageing history, contact log and missed terms into a write-off referral package

sipoc.input: Commitment Due-Date Monitoring Outcome [Grace Period Not Exhausted]
sipoc.process: Analyst extends the monitoring window by the remaining grace allowance and re-queues the commitment for the next remittance cycle check

data.label: Broken Promise-to-Pay Record, Referred for Write-off Assessment

provisions-writeoff.name: Provisions & Write-off
provisions-writeoff.description: Provisions & Write-off is the CPG AR governance checkpoint that dispositions balances the collections cadence could not recover: the Collections Analyst compiles a case file from the ageing history, dunning log and broken promise-to-pay record, scores recoverability against ageing bucket, payment history and collateral status to classify the balance as doubtful or uncollectable, and prepares the corresponding provision-reserve or write-off proposal; the Credit & Collections Manager screens the proposal against policy and approves it directly within delegated authority or escalates above-threshold cases to the Finance Controller for sign-off, with declined requests returned to active collections rather than written off; once approved, the AR Controller posts the write-off or provision entry and reconciles the open-item ledger so the cleared position is ready for incoming remittance matching in Payment Identification.

data.label: Broken Promise-to-Pay Record, Referred for Write-off Assessment

sipoc.input: Broken Promise-to-Pay Record, Referred for Write-off Assessment
sipoc.process: Analyst assembles ageing history, dunning log, dispute status and prior promise-to-pay attempts into a single write-off and provision case file
Analyst applies the recoverability scoring model, weighing ageing bucket, customer payment history and collateral or guarantee status, to classify the balance as Doubtful or Uncollectable
Analyst drafts the full write-off proposal citing exhausted collections effort, ageing bucket and recoverability score against policy thresholds
Analyst drafts a doubtful-debt reserve proposal, sizing the provision against the ageing bucket and residual recovery probability
Manager screens proposal completeness, recoverability score and prior collections effort against policy before routing to the correct approval tier
Manager approves the write-off or provision directly under standard delegation-of-authority limits, for example up to $25K
Manager forwards above-threshold cases with full case documentation to the Finance Controller for higher-tier sign-off
Finance Controller authorises the above-threshold write-off or provision, recording accounting-period and reserve-impact rationale

data.label: Return Case for Renewed Collections Effort

sipoc.process: Analyst receives the declined case and reinstates the account into active collections cadence rather than writing off the balance
AR Controller posts the approved write-off, relieving AR, or the doubtful-debt provision, booking the reserve, against the customer's open items per period-close policy

data.label: Write-off Request Declined, Returned to Active Collections Cadence

sipoc.process: Controller reconciles the adjusted open-item ledger to confirm the written-off or provisioned balance is removed from active dunning and remaining items are clean for remittance matching

data.label: AR Open Item Ledger Reconciled, Ready for Payment Application

payment-identification.name: Payment Identification
payment-identification.description: In this CPG cash-application AS-IS process, the Treasury Operations Analyst receives an incoming bank statement payment line from the corporate lockbox or virtual-account feed and extracts payer name, amount, value date, and bank reference fields before handing the record to the Cash Application Specialist, who cross-references EDI 820 or emailed remittance advice to capture invoice-level pay-against detail; when remittance detail is incomplete the Cash Application Analyst dispatches a clarification inquiry to the customer AP contact or Treasury Operations Analyst and awaits a response, escalating a chronically unresponsive inquiry to a parked status once an aging threshold is reached, while complete remittance detail proceeds through customer account coding, currency and value-date validation against the treasury cash position, and a duplicate-payment screen by the Cash Application Manager that either suspends a suspected duplicate pending bank-side confirmation or releases a finalized identified remittance record into payment matching and reconciliation.

sipoc.process: parses the BAI2/MT940 lockbox statement line to isolate payer name, amount, value date, and reference number fields
cross-references EDI 820 or emailed remittance advice against the extracted data to capture invoice-level pay-against detail
matches payer name and bank reference against the customer master to assign the paying customer account code
issues a remittance clarification request to the customer AP contact or Treasury Operations Analyst citing the missing invoice reference or amount breakdown
validates payment currency, gross amount, and bank value date against the treasury cash position feed
monitors the customer portal and shared inbox for the incoming remittance clarification reply within the service-level window
flags the record to the Treasury Operations Analyst for bank-side duplicate confirmation and holds release pending confirmation
consolidates the coded and validated payment data into a finalized identified-payment record ready for matching queue release

payment-matching-reconciliation.name: Payment Matching & Reconciliation
payment-matching-reconciliation.description: Building on the identified remittance record released from payment identification, the Cash Application Specialist runs the ERP auto-cash matching algorithm against open AR sub-ledger invoices using exact invoice number, customer account, and amount-tolerance rules; exact system matches are confirmed by the Cash Application Specialist and handed to the AR Reconciliation Lead, while unmatched lines are searched by the Cash Application Analyst across the open invoice ledger by customer account, date range, and part-amount combinations, with candidate invoices manually matched and reason-coded, and remittances lacking any candidate invoice routed through a clarification-request and aging-threshold loop with the customer AP or sales order desk before being parked as unresolved; all matched items are then reconciled to the AR sub-ledger balance by the AR Reconciliation Lead, with the Cash Application Manager screening residual variance against tolerance so that fully agreeing items are finalized with no variance while any residual variance is packaged as a reconciled item ready for deduction identification.

sipoc.process: executes the ERP auto-cash matching algorithm against open AR sub-ledger invoices using exact invoice number, customer account, and amount-tolerance rules
verifies matched invoice amount, terms, and customer account against remittance detail to confirm the system-proposed match
interrogates the open AR sub-ledger by customer account, date range, and part-amount combinations to locate candidate invoices for the unmatched remittance
links the remittance amount to the selected open invoice applying a manual match reason code and part-payment allocation
sends a clarification request to the customer AP contact or sales order desk to confirm the invoice reference for the unmatched amount
ties the matched cash item to the AR sub-ledger open-item balance, confirming residual balance equals zero or identifying a variance amount requiring tolerance check
tracks the customer portal and email inbox for the reference confirmation reply within the aging service-level window
closes out the reconciliation record confirming invoice and payment amounts fully agree with no residual balance

data.label: Finalized Matched Cash Item - No Variance

deduction-identification.name: Deduction Identification
deduction-identification.description: Opening on a reconciled cash item carrying a residual variance from payment matching and reconciliation, the Deductions Analyst screens the variance amount and the customer's deduction history against the reason-code taxonomy - trade promotion, shortage, pricing, or chargeback - to assign a preliminary deduction category, then validates supporting proof-of-delivery, promotion agreement, or debit memo backup where attached, or dispatches a documentation request to the customer deductions contact or internal trade promotion team and cycles through an await-and-aging-threshold loop when backup is missing; the AR Reconciliation Lead confirms the deduction amount against the trade-spend accrual or agreed pricing terms, and the Deductions Manager either pursues repayment on an unsupported amount ruled worth disputing or accepts it as a courtesy deduction under customer-tier policy, with the Deductions Analyst compiling the final reason code, amount, and reference into an identified deduction record ready for cash posting and adjustment.

sipoc.process: screens the variance amount and customer deduction history against the reason-code taxonomy - trade promotion, shortage, pricing, chargeback - to assign a preliminary deduction category
cross-checks the proof-of-delivery, promotion agreement, or debit memo against the claimed deduction amount and date to confirm supporting evidence
sends a documentation request to the customer deductions contact or internal trade promotion team for the debit memo, POD, or pricing agreement backup
monitors the customer deduction portal and shared mailbox for the incoming backup attachment within the aging service window
confirms the deduction amount reconciles to the trade spend accrual or agreed pricing terms and approves it as a legitimate customer deduction
flags the deduction as unsupported against the accrual or pricing master and routes it to the Deductions Manager for a dispute decision
accepts the unsupported amount as a courtesy deduction per customer-tier policy and reclassifies it under the applicable reason code
compiles the final deduction reason code, amount, and supporting reference into the deduction record ready for cash posting and adjustment

cash-posting-adjustment.name: Cash Posting & Adjustment
cash-posting-adjustment.description: Receiving the identified deduction record from deduction identification, the Cash Application Specialist assembles the reconciled cash item and deduction record into an ERP cash-application posting batch with GL account and cost-center coding and posts it to clear the open invoice balance, cycling a batch-reject error such as an account block, currency mismatch, or closed period through a correction request and aging-threshold loop with the Cash Application Analyst before parking an unresolved exception; once accepted, the AR Reconciliation Lead posts the accepted deduction as a write-off or trade-accrual offset adjustment against the corresponding GL accrual account, and the Cash Application Manager screens accrual coverage so that a fully covered adjustment closes the open item while an amount exceeding available accrual is packaged with its reason code and supporting backup into an identified short-pay record ready for claims registration.

sipoc.process: assembles the reconciled cash item and deduction record into the ERP cash-application posting batch with GL account and cost-center coding
submits the batch to the ERP AR sub-ledger to clear the open invoice balance against the identified matched cash amount
posts the accepted deduction amount as a write-off or trade-accrual offset adjustment entry against the corresponding GL accrual account
routes the ERP batch-reject error - account block, currency mismatch, closed period - to the Cash Application Analyst for correction of the posting reference
monitors the ERP batch queue and analyst inbox for the corrected account coding or reopened period confirmation
confirms the accrual offset fully clears the open-item balance and closes the invoice line in the AR sub-ledger
compiles the residual short-pay amount, deduction reason code, and supporting backup into a claim-ready package exceeding available trade accrual

data.label: Cash Entry Parked - Posting Exception Unresolved

claims-registration.name: Claims Registration
claims-registration.description: Claims Registration is the front-line intake process within CPG Order-to-Cash where deductions and short-pays isolated during cash posting are formally classified, reason-coded against the customer's remittance backup and EDI 812/820 chargeback codes, and logged as discrete claim records in the deductions register; the Deductions Analyst hands off the unauthorized withholding to a Claims Registration Clerk who determines whether it reflects a shortage, transit damage, pricing discrepancy, or promotional trade-spend claim, escalating unidentifiable reason codes to a Trade Claims Analyst who solicits backup documentation from the customer's deduction team before the item can be categorized, registered under a unique claim ID, and assigned by the Claims Manager to a Trade Claims Analyst for downstream validation.

sipoc.process: Analyst isolates the unauthorized deduction from the remittance advice, matching the short-pay amount against the open invoice balance to confirm a claim scenario exists rather than a cash-application posting error

Clerk reviews the customer's remittance backup, EDI 812/820 codes, and chargeback memo to assign a standardized reason code (shortage, damage, pricing, or promotional trade-spend) per the claims taxonomy

Analyst applies the claims taxonomy to assign a formal claim-type category — shortage, damage, pricing, or promotional trade-spend — determining which substantiation criteria will govern downstream validation

Trade Claims Analyst issues a documentation request to the customer's deduction team or broker requesting proof of delivery, damage photos, or promotional agreement backup within a 15-business-day service window

Clerk creates a formal claim record in the deductions management system, capturing customer, invoice reference, deduction amount, reason code, and category, and generates a unique claim ID for tracking

Analyst monitors the shared deduction portal and email inbox for customer-submitted proof of delivery, bill-of-lading exceptions, or trade-fund agreements, logging each response against the claim aging clock
Manager reviews the claim register queue and assigns each newly logged claim to a Trade Claims Analyst based on customer portfolio and claim-category workload balancing

data.label: Assigned Claim Record — Ready for Validation

claims-validation.name: Claims Validation
claims-validation.description: Claims Validation is the substantiation stage where a Claims Validation Analyst retrieves the assigned claim file and its supporting backup, cross-references proof-of-delivery and warehouse receiving exceptions against the claimed shortage or damage quantity, and — for pricing and promotional trade-spend claims — routes the file to a Sales Finance Analyst who tests the claimed amount against the signed trade agreement and approved accrual rate on the off-invoice allowance ledger; claims lacking sufficient substantiation trigger a Trade Claims Analyst follow-up request for supplemental evidence governed by an aging clock that either resolves the gap or times out into rejection, while adequately substantiated claims proceed to a Claims Manager adjudication verdict that either invalidates the claim for recovery or finalizes a validated claim package routed forward to approval.

data.label: Assigned Claim Record — Ready for Validation

sipoc.input: Assigned Claim Record — Ready for Validation
sipoc.process: Analyst pulls the claim file from the deductions register and retrieves the attached backup — proof of delivery, damage photos, promotional agreement, or price list — from the shared document repository

Analyst cross-references the carrier's proof-of-delivery record and warehouse receiving exception report against the claimed shortage or damage quantity to confirm the discrepancy occurred in transit or at receipt

For pricing and trade-spend claims, the Sales Finance Analyst cross-references the claimed amount against the signed promotional agreement, approved accrual rate, and off-invoice allowance ledger to confirm contractual entitlement

Analyst applies the claims adjudication matrix — comparing substantiated amount to claimed amount, checking for duplicate claim numbers, and verifying against the customer's historical deduction pattern — to render a valid, partially valid, or invalid verdict

Trade Claims Analyst contacts the customer's deduction team or internal sales rep requesting the missing bill-of-lading, weight-and-inspection certificate, or promotional sign-off within a defined response window
Analyst monitors the customer portal and email for submission of the requested supplemental evidence, logging receipt date against the claim aging clock
Manager reviews the substantiation file and validity verdict, appends the recommended settlement amount, and compiles the final validated package for the approval workflow

data.label: Validated Claim File — Routed for Approval

claims-approval.name: Claims Approval
claims-approval.description: Claims Approval applies the CPG trade-claims delegation-of-authority matrix, routing the Trade Claims Analyst's approval recommendation memo through tiered sign-off — a Claims Manager for claims within a lower dollar threshold, a Trade Finance Controller for mid-tier exposure, and a Finance Director for high-value exceptions reviewed in the monthly claims forum — with insufficiently justified high-value claims looped back to the Trade Claims Analyst for supplemental business-justification evidence from the sales account team before a final aging threshold either forces rejection or allows re-adjudication, and with all authorized tiers converging on a single consolidated approval authorization record that is ready for settlement disbursement.

data.label: Validated Claim File — Routed for Approval

sipoc.input: Validated Claim File — Routed for Approval
sipoc.process: Analyst drafts an approval recommendation memo summarizing the substantiated deduction amount, claim category, customer history, and recommended settlement action for the approving authority
Manager reviews the recommendation memo against the delegated approval matrix limit and signs off on claims within the $10,000 manager authorization threshold

Controller receives the escalated claim package and reviews the substantiation file, trade-spend budget impact, and customer profitability tier before applying the higher controller authorization threshold
Controller formally authorizes the claim within the $75,000 controller threshold and records the approval decision and rationale in the claims workflow system
Finance Director reviews claims exceeding the controller threshold in the monthly high-value claims forum, weighing customer relationship impact, trade-spend budget exposure, and precedent risk
Director ratifies the final approval decision across all escalation tiers, consolidating the authorization record and setting the approved settlement amount for disbursement processing
Analyst requests supplemental business justification from the sales account team — margin-impact analysis, competitive-risk notes, or customer scorecard data — to support the escalated claim

Analyst consolidates the tier-specific approval record — whether authorized at manager, controller, or director level — into a single authorization file, verifying the delegation-of-authority signature and approved settlement amount are correctly recorded
Analyst tracks the sales account team's submission of supplemental justification against the claim aging clock and logs the response for re-adjudication

data.label: Approved Claim Ruling — Ready for Settlement

claims-settlement.name: Claims Settlement
claims-settlement.description: Claims Settlement, the terminal step of the Order-to-Cash cycle, has the Trade Finance Controller determine whether the approved claim ruling disburses as a customer credit memo or as a debit-memo recovery for claims found overstated, with a Claims Resolution Specialist issuing the corresponding instrument and a Deductions Analyst applying credits against the open short-pay or matching recovery payments against debit memos — escalating undisputed non-payment to collections and routing disputed debit memos through a substantiation request-and-aging loop that either upholds the recovery for legal referral or returns it to the recovery track — while an Accounts Receivable Analyst reconciles the settled position to the AR sub-ledger before the Trade Finance Controller formally closes the claim record, clearing the deduction and completing the O2C cycle.

data.label: Approved Claim Ruling — Ready for Settlement

sipoc.input: Approved Claim Ruling — Ready for Settlement
sipoc.process: Controller determines whether the approved claim settles via customer credit memo, direct debit memo for claims found invalid or overstated, or write-off against the trade-spend accrual, based on the claim category and approval ruling terms

Specialist generates a credit memo in the billing system referencing the original invoice and claim ID, routes it through the standard credit approval workflow, and releases it to the customer's account

For claims determined invalid or overstated during validation, the specialist issues a debit memo or recovery invoice back to the customer for the disputed portion and forwards it to the collections queue for follow-up

Analyst applies the issued credit memo against the corresponding open short-pay deduction line in the customer's AR sub-ledger, matching amount and reference number to clear the exact deduction balance

Specialist requests the customer submit substantiation for the dispute — alternate proof of delivery or corrected invoice reference — within a 10-business-day window before the debit memo is finalized
Analyst monitors the customer's remittance activity for payment against the outstanding debit memo within the standard 30-day collection window
Specialist monitors for the customer's dispute substantiation submission via the deduction portal and logs receipt against the dispute resolution aging clock

data.label: Match Recovery Payment to Debit Memo

sipoc.process: Analyst matches the incoming recovery payment against the outstanding debit memo in the AR sub-ledger, confirming amount and clearing the recovery receivable
Analyst escalates the unpaid debit memo recovery beyond the 30-day follow-up window to the credit and collections team for continued dunning and potential future deduction offset
Analyst reconciles the settled claim amount against the AR sub-ledger and open deduction aging report, confirming the deduction line no longer appears as an unresolved short-pay

data.label: Claim Recovery Disputed — Referred to Collections/Legal
Close Claim Record and Report Resolution

sipoc.process: Controller reviews the reconciled settlement record, formally closes the claim in the claims management system with a final disposition code and cycle-time metrics, and reports the resolution in the monthly trade-spend and deductions summary

vendor-registration-validation.name: Vendor Registration & Validation
vendor-registration-validation.description: Vendor Registration & Validation is the entry gate of the CPG procure-to-pay vendor master lifecycle, triggered whenever a category buyer identifies a supply gap that cannot be filled by an existing approved supplier and raises a new-vendor request; the buyer documents the business justification, spend estimate, and incumbent gap rationale before handing the request to the Vendor Master Data Analyst, who keys the legal entity name, tax identification number, registered address, and business classification into the vendor master intake screen and chases any missing certificate of incorporation, tax residency form, or trade license through a bounded documentation-request and follow-up cycle that ultimately suspends chronically non-responsive requests; once the legal entity paperwork is complete, the analyst cross-references the registered name and registration number against the national business registry and D-U-N-S database, the Procurement Compliance Analyst confirms the registry match, and the analyst assigns a unique vendor master number and stages the record as pending bank verification for the next stage.
