sipoc.process: Buyer documents the business need, category spend estimate, and incumbent supply gap driving the new vendor request
Analyst keys the legal entity name, tax identification number, registered address, and business classification into the vendor master intake screen
Analyst cross-references the legal name and registration number against the national business registry and D-U-N-S database to confirm the entity is active and matches submitted documents
Analyst issues a standardized request listing the missing certificate of incorporation, tax residency form, or trade license and logs the 10-business-day response SLA
Buyer monitors the vendor portal and email inbox for the outstanding certificate of incorporation, tax form, or trade license within the SLA window
Analyst generates the unique vendor master number in the vendor master file and sets the record status to Pending Bank Verification
sipoc.output: Vendor Master Record — Pending Bank Verification

data.label: Vendor Master Record — Pending Bank Verification

bank-detail-verification.name: Bank Detail Verification
bank-detail-verification.description: Bank Detail Verification picks up the freshly registered vendor master record and protects the organization from payment-diversion fraud by independently confirming that the banking instructions on file truly belong to the registered legal entity; the Vendor Master Data Analyst requests a voided check, bank letter, or IBAN confirmation, the AP Vendor Team Lead logs the submission and chases missing evidence through a bounded reminder cycle that suspends non-responsive vendors, and once evidence is in hand the team lead validates that the bank letter's named account holder matches the registered legal entity before the Procurement Compliance Analyst performs an outbound callback to the bank's own published corporate treasury line, never a number supplied by the vendor, to confirm the account is open and controlled by the vendor; a confirmed callback lets the analyst load the verified banking details onto the vendor master and hand the record forward staged as pending compliance screening, while any ownership mismatch is rejected outright as a likely fraud indicator.

data.label: Vendor Master Record — Pending Bank Verification

sipoc.input: Vendor Master Record — Pending Bank Verification
sipoc.process: Analyst sends the vendor a secure portal link requesting a voided check, bank letter, or IBAN confirmation matching the registered legal entity name
Team lead logs the submitted account number, routing or IBAN code, and bank letter into the bank detail change queue
Team lead confirms the bank letter's named account holder matches the registered legal entity name and the account is active with the issuing bank
Team lead sends a follow-up reminder citing the specific missing voided check or bank letter and restates the 5-business-day resubmission window
Analyst tracks the vendor portal for the resubmitted voided check or bank letter within the reminder window
Analyst places an outbound callback to the bank's own published corporate treasury line, never a number supplied by the vendor, to confirm the account is open and controlled by the vendor

data.label: Activate Bank Detail on Vendor Master

sipoc.process: Analyst loads the verified bank account and routing details onto the vendor master file and advances the record status to Pending Compliance Screening
sipoc.output: Vendor Master Record — Bank Verified, Pending Compliance Screening

data.label: Vendor Record Suspended — Non-Responsive on Bank Evidence
Vendor Master Record — Bank Verified, Pending Compliance Screening

compliance-risk-screening.name: Compliance & Risk Screening
compliance-risk-screening.description: Compliance & Risk Screening takes the bank-verified vendor record and subjects it to sanctions, ownership, and financial risk due diligence before the vendor is allowed to transact; the Vendor Master Data Analyst compiles a screening packet including the beneficial ownership declaration and chases any insufficient ownership disclosure through a bounded follow-up cycle that can suspend the onboarding, after which the Procurement Compliance Analyst screens the legal entity and all beneficial owners against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists and adverse media, terminating outright on any confirmed hit; a clean screen leads to a financial risk score built from credit bureau data, years in business, and litigation history, the Category Buyer attests that no employee or related party holds an interest in the vendor, and risk scores above threshold are escalated to the Compliance Manager for a documented risk-acceptance decision, with denial ending the onboarding; approved or in-threshold vendors are assigned a compliance risk tier and re-screening date, and the record is stamped compliance-cleared and handed to ongoing vendor maintenance for final activation.

data.label: Vendor Master Record — Bank Verified, Pending Compliance Screening

sipoc.input: Vendor Master Record — Bank Verified, Pending Compliance Screening
sipoc.process: Analyst assembles the legal entity name, registered address, and beneficial ownership declaration into a screening packet for the compliance queue
Analyst runs the legal entity name and all beneficial owners against OFAC, EU, and UN sanctions lists plus adverse media watchlists
Analyst requests the vendor's beneficial ownership disclosure form identifying all parties holding 25 percent or greater equity
Analyst monitors the vendor portal for the completed beneficial ownership disclosure form within the 7-business-day window
Analyst pulls the vendor's credit bureau score, years in business, and litigation history to calculate a financial risk rating

data.label: Vendor Onboarding Suspended — Ownership Disclosure Non-Responsive

sipoc.process: Buyer confirms in writing that no employee, family member, or related party holds an ownership or compensation interest in the vendor
Analyst packages the elevated risk score, adverse media notes, and attestation for a documented manager risk-acceptance decision
Analyst records the assigned low, medium, or high risk tier and next re-screening date on the vendor compliance profile

data.label: Vendor Onboarding Rejected — Risk Exception Denied

sipoc.process: Analyst sets the vendor master status field to Compliance Cleared and stamps the next scheduled re-screening date
sipoc.output: Vendor Master Record — Cleared for Activation

data.label: Vendor Master Record — Cleared for Activation

ongoing-vendor-maintenance.name: Ongoing Vendor Maintenance
ongoing-vendor-maintenance.description: Ongoing Vendor Maintenance closes out the onboarding journey and establishes the recurring master-data hygiene that keeps an active vendor transactable; the Vendor Master Data Analyst flips the compliance-cleared record to active status and enables it for purchase order and invoice matching, then screens the new record against the existing vendor master file for duplicates by fuzzy-matching legal name, tax ID, and remit-to address, reconciling or merging any potential match and terminating the onboarding outright if the vendor is confirmed as a pre-existing duplicate; the Category Buyer then confirms that negotiated payment terms, currency, and catalog scope are correctly reflected on the live record, and the analyst checks for an on-file W-9 or W-8BEN tax reporting form needed for 1099 payment reporting, chasing any missing form through a bounded request-and-reminder cycle with the AP Vendor Team Lead that places the vendor on payment hold if it goes unanswered; once terms are confirmed and tax reporting is on file, the analyst performs a final completeness check across registration, bank, compliance, and tax fields and stamps the vendor master record as active and ready to transact, handing it forward into sourcing event setup.

data.label: Vendor Master Record — Cleared for Activation

sipoc.input: Vendor Master Record — Cleared for Activation
sipoc.process: Analyst sets the vendor master status flag to Active, enables the vendor for purchase order and invoice matching, and notifies the requesting buyer
Analyst runs a fuzzy-match scan of the legal name, tax ID, and remit-to address against existing active vendor master records to detect duplicates
Analyst compares the two vendor master records field by field and either merges the new record into the existing vendor number or documents the distinguishing entity attributes
Buyer confirms the negotiated payment terms, currency, and item or service catalog scope are correctly reflected on the vendor record

data.label: Vendor Onboarding Terminated — Duplicate of Existing Vendor

sipoc.input: Buyer-Confirmed Vendor Terms [Tax Form on File]
sipoc.process: Analyst performs a final master data completeness check across registration, bank, compliance, and tax fields and stamps the record ready for sourcing activity
sipoc.output: Active Vendor Master Record — Ready to Transact

sipoc.process: Analyst requests the outstanding W-9 or W-8BEN tax reporting form required to enable 1099 payment reporting

data.label: Active Vendor Master Record — Ready to Transact

sipoc.process: Team lead monitors the vendor portal for the completed tax reporting form within the 10-business-day window

data.label: Vendor Payment Hold — Tax Reporting Form Non-Responsive

sourcing-event-setup.name: Sourcing Event Setup
sourcing-event-setup.description: In a CPG procurement organization, Sourcing Event Setup begins once Ongoing Vendor Maintenance has delivered a current approved vendor pool and a category demand signal has been raised from S&OP or a contract expiry trigger, and the Category Sourcing Manager scopes the addressable spend, lot structure and specification requirements before the Procurement Analyst segments the category into discrete bid lots by SKU family, region or volume tier to preserve competitive tension and mitigate single-source risk; the Sourcing Buyer then drafts technical and commercial RFQ specifications referencing category templates and prior award benchmarks, after which the manager checks whether the approved vendor pool has sufficient qualified capacity per lot, looping the buyer through supplemental supplier qualification and a qualification-threshold check when coverage is thin before either cancelling the event for an unworkable vendor pool or proceeding to publish the finalized RFQ package to the qualified pool with evaluation weightings and deadlines, culminating in Procurement Director sign-off that authorizes the sourcing event launch and hands a live, bid-soliciting event to Bid Evaluation & Award.

sipoc.input: Approved Vendor Pool & Category Demand Signal
sipoc.process: Manager interrogates category spend history, forecasted demand volume and specification requirements against the approved vendor pool's category coverage to define the sourcing event scope and lot structure

Analyst segments total addressable category spend into discrete bid lots by SKU family, plant region and volume tier so that no single lot exceeds a concentration threshold that would erode competitive tension
Buyer drafts technical specifications, quality standards, packaging and delivery terms per lot, referencing the category RFQ template library and pricing benchmarks from the two prior award cycles

Buyer finalizes the RFQ package with approved specifications, disclosed evaluation criteria weightings of 60 percent commercial and 40 percent technical, and submission deadlines, then distributes it via the sourcing portal to the qualified vendor pool

Buyer dispatches qualification requests to candidate suppliers identified via the supplier database and category market scan to close capability or capacity gaps against the minimum three-bidder-per-lot policy

Director reviews estimated addressable spend, evaluation criteria weightings and vendor pool diversity against the category sourcing strategy and delegation-of-authority thresholds before authorizing the event to go live

Analyst monitors the qualification queue, tracking outstanding insurance certificates, quality audit results and compliance documentation submitted by candidate suppliers against the category onboarding checklist

bid-evaluation-award.name: Bid Evaluation & Award
bid-evaluation-award.description: Once Sourcing Event Setup has published the RFQ and opened bid solicitation to the qualified vendor pool, Bid Evaluation & Award has the Sourcing Buyer collect and consolidate incoming bid submissions and confirm a minimum-bidder threshold is met per lot, looping through solicitation reminders and a deadline-extension check when responses are thin before either cancelling the event or handing compliant bids to the Procurement Analyst to screen against mandatory requirements such as insurance certificates and pricing completeness; the Category Sourcing Manager then scores each compliant bid against weighted technical criteria including quality specification, capacity and sustainability credentials, the analyst blends in commercial scoring on landed cost, payment terms and volume rebates, and the buyer tabulates ranked results and flags statistical outliers, which the manager adjudicates when anomalies are detected before the Procurement Director reviews the ranked and adjudicated tabulation against category savings targets and approves the supplier shortlist that Negotiation & Savings will carry into bargaining.

sipoc.process: Buyer collects incoming bid submissions through the sourcing portal deadline window, logging lot coverage and flagging suppliers in the qualified pool who have not yet responded

Analyst screens each submitted bid against mandatory compliance requirements including insurance certification, mandatory specification adherence and pricing completeness, disqualifying non-conforming bids
Buyer dispatches a reminder and short deadline extension to non-responding qualified suppliers per lot to reach the category policy minimum of three competitive bids

Manager scores each compliant bid against weighted technical criteria worth 40 percent of the total score, covering quality specification, production capacity, lead time and sustainability credentials per the category scorecard model
Analyst monitors the extended submission window, updating the bid count per lot against the minimum-bidder policy as late responses arrive

Analyst scores commercial dimensions worth 60 percent of the total score, covering unit price, total landed cost, payment terms and volume rebate tiers, then blends the result with the technical score
Buyer tabulates ranked bid results across all lots, cross-referencing scorecard weightings and flagging statistical pricing outliers or scoring discrepancies for adjudication

Manager convenes an adjudication review with the analyst and buyer to resolve scoring discrepancies, disqualify non-conforming outlier bids, or validate unusually low pricing before finalizing the ranking
Director reviews the ranked and adjudicated bid tabulation against category savings targets and supplier risk diversification policy to approve which suppliers advance into negotiation per lot

negotiation-savings.name: Negotiation & Savings
negotiation-savings.description: Taking the Procurement Director's approved supplier shortlist from Bid Evaluation & Award, Negotiation & Savings opens with the Category Sourcing Manager setting a per-lot savings target and walk-away ceiling against the category budget baseline, after which the Sourcing Buyer prepares a lot-specific negotiation playbook sequencing concessions and leverage points drawn from the bid tabulation before conducting structured negotiation rounds with each shortlisted supplier on price, volume commitment, payment terms and service levels; the Procurement Analyst models the total-cost-of-ownership impact of each supplier counteroffer against the savings target, and when the gap remains open the manager triggers a revised counter-proposal cycle with a round-extension threshold check that either loops back for another round of analysis or removes the supplier from award consideration, while suppliers who close the gap have their terms finalized by the buyer into a negotiated terms sheet that the Procurement Director certifies against the savings baseline before releasing it to Supplier Selection.

sipoc.process: Manager establishes a target savings percentage and a walk-away price ceiling per lot, referencing the category budget baseline and prior-year contract benchmarks for the shortlisted suppliers

Buyer prepares a lot-specific negotiation playbook detailing concession sequencing, leverage points sourced from the bid tabulation, and a best-alternative-to-negotiated-agreement position per shortlisted supplier

Buyer conducts a structured negotiation session with the shortlisted supplier, presenting counter-targets on unit price, volume commitment, payment terms and service levels in the sequence set by the playbook
Analyst models the total-cost-of-ownership impact of the supplier's counteroffer against the savings target and budget baseline, quantifying the remaining gap to target
Buyer finalizes the negotiated terms sheet capturing agreed unit pricing, volume tiers, payment terms and service-level commitments per supplier, formatted for legal contract handoff
Buyer dispatches a revised counter-proposal adjusting the next concession lever in the playbook's escalation path and requests a supplier response by the round deadline
Director reviews the achieved savings against the target baseline and delegation-of-authority thresholds, certifying the commercial terms before advancing the supplier to final selection
Analyst tracks the outstanding supplier response against the negotiation round deadline and logs any incoming revised terms for re-analysis

supplier-selection.name: Supplier Selection
supplier-selection.description: With negotiated terms finalized and certified by Negotiation & Savings, Supplier Selection has the Category Sourcing Manager compile an award recommendation package consolidating negotiated terms sheets, savings certification and supplier risk profiles per lot, which the Procurement Analyst validates against category risk policy on financial stability, compliance status and sustainability scorecard, looping the Sourcing Buyer through supplementary risk documentation requests and a remediation-deadline check for flagged suppliers before either disqualifying an out-of-policy supplier in favor of the next-ranked bidder or letting the manager finalize the in-policy finalist list; the buyer then prepares an award decision brief summarizing recommended suppliers, negotiated pricing and residual risk for executive sign-off, and the Procurement Director either approves the final supplier award, which the manager formally notifies to winning and losing bidders to close the event and hand a selected supplier and award decision to Contract Authoring, or rejects the recommendation and returns it for renegotiation of terms.

sipoc.process: Manager compiles the award recommendation package consolidating negotiated terms sheets, savings certification and preliminary supplier risk notes for each shortlisted lot into a single decision package
Analyst validates each candidate supplier's financial stability rating, regulatory compliance status and sustainability scorecard against the category risk policy thresholds for award eligibility
Manager finalizes the finalist supplier list per lot, cross-referencing validated risk profiles against negotiated savings and technical scores to confirm award readiness

sipoc.input: Supplier Risk Validation Report [Risk Flag Raised]
sipoc.process: Buyer dispatches a request to the flagged supplier for supplementary financial statements, compliance certificates or a remediation plan to close the identified risk gap
Buyer prepares the award decision brief summarizing the recommended supplier per lot, negotiated pricing, savings delivered versus target, and residual risk items for executive sign-off
Analyst tracks incoming remediation documentation against the response deadline and re-validates it against the category risk policy checklist upon receipt
Director formally approves the final supplier award per lot within delegation-of-authority limits and instructs the category manager to notify winning and losing bidders
Manager notifies the winning supplier of the award and unsuccessful bidders of the outcome per the category debrief policy, formally closing the competitive sourcing event

contract-authoring.name: Contract Authoring
contract-authoring.description: In a CPG procure-to-pay contract lifecycle, Contract Authoring begins the moment Supplier Selection closes with a signed award decision and ends when a fully negotiated draft is endorsed and ready for internal approval routing; the Procurement Category Manager translates the award's commercial terms into a structured terms brief, the Contract Manager populates the appropriate master template by spend category and supplier risk tier, Procurement Legal Counsel interrogates indemnification, liability, termination-for-convenience, and IP clauses against policy thresholds, and any unresolved redlines are clarified through bounded escalation rounds before the Contract Manager finalizes the draft and the Category Manager endorses the commercial terms for signature routing, with persistent legal impasses terminating authoring outright rather than stalling the pipeline.

sipoc.process: extract negotiated pricing tiers, volume rebates, and payment terms from the award decision and package them into a structured terms brief for contract drafting
select the template by spend category and supplier risk tier, then insert commercial terms, SLAs, and volume commitments from the terms brief
interrogate indemnification caps, liability limits, termination-for-convenience, and IP ownership clauses against risk-tier policy
incorporate the cleared redlines into a clean final draft, reconcile commercial terms with the terms brief, and format for signature routing
confirm pricing, volume commitments, and rebate structure match the negotiated award terms and business case
log unresolved clause objections and route targeted clarification questions back to legal counsel with a response deadline

data.label: Category-Endorsed Draft Contract Ready for Signature

sipoc.process: research precedent and policy exceptions, then issue revised clause language or clause acceptance

approval-signature.name: Approval & Signature
approval-signature.description: Approval & Signature picks up the category-endorsed draft contract and drives it through delegation-of-authority adjudication and bilateral execution before compliance monitoring can begin; the Contract Manager assembles the routing packet and Procurement Legal Counsel certifies that all negotiated redlines are incorporated, the Delegated Approval Authority adjudicates within or escalates beyond their signing tier, approved contracts are internally signed off and dispatched for supplier countersignature with escalating reminders against a non-responsiveness threshold, and only a fully countersigned, catalog-activated contract is handed to the Procurement Category Manager and Contract Compliance Analyst as an operational, monitorable asset.

sipoc.process: compile the signature packet with redline history, delegation-of-authority tier calculation from contract value and term length, and risk commentary
confirm all negotiated redlines and risk exceptions are incorporated in the final packet and certify legal sign-off before signature routing

data.label: Escalate Packet to Higher Authority Tier

sipoc.input: Legally Certified Routing Packet [Exceeds Delegated Authority]
sipoc.process: reroute the packet with an executive briefing note to the next delegation-of-authority tier per the contract value threshold
apply internal signature and authorization per delegation-of-authority policy and timestamp the approval record
transmit the final contract via the e-signature platform to the supplier's authorized signatory with an execution deadline
validate both-party signatures, register the final document in the contract repository, and notify category management
load contract terms into the source-to-pay catalog and CLM system flags and notify the compliance analyst of monitoring obligations
issue a formal reminder notice citing the contractual execution deadline and escalate to the supplier relationship contact

data.label: Activated Executed Contract Ready for Monitoring

compliance-monitoring.name: Compliance Monitoring
compliance-monitoring.description: Compliance Monitoring converts an activated, executed contract into a continuously governed asset until it is either confirmed renewal-eligible or terminated for cause; the Contract Compliance Analyst extracts an obligation calendar covering SLA thresholds, insurance certificate dates, and rebate triggers, captures supplier performance against contracted service levels, and where thresholds are missed logs a compliance exception that the Procurement Category Manager escalates through bounded cure-notice cycles with the Contract Manager before a persistent breach terminates the contract for cause, while in parallel the Analyst reconciles actual spend against contracted volume commitments, redirecting any maverick spend back onto contract before certifying the period's compliance status as renewal-eligible.

sipoc.process: parse the executed contract for SLA thresholds, insurance certificate renewal dates, rebate tier triggers, and quarterly reporting obligations
aggregate scorecarded on-time-in-full delivery, quality reject rate, and fill-rate metrics against contracted SLA thresholds for the period
document breach specifics against contracted service levels, calculate service credits or penalty per the remedies clause, and open an exception case
match actual invoiced and purchased volume against contracted volume commitments to identify off-contract maverick spend leakage for the period

data.label: Contract Terminated for Cause — Persistent Non-Compliance

sipoc.process: issue a formal cure notice per the breach clause citing specific SLA failures, service credit assessment, and remediation deadline
notify requisitioning teams of contract leakage, redirect future purchase orders to the awarded contract, and log the recovery savings estimate
track supplier acknowledgment and collect a remediation commitment or corrective action plan against the cure deadline
certify SLA compliance, spend-under-contract status, and obligation calendar currency for the period, then publish status to category management and flag renewal eligibility

data.label: Compliance Status Confirmation Record — Renewal Eligible

renewal-expiry.name: Renewal & Expiry
renewal-expiry.description: Renewal & Expiry closes the contract lifecycle loop, converting a compliance-confirmed, renewal-eligible contract into either an active renewed catalog entry that re-enables downstream Requisition Intake or a routed exit to re-bid or sunset; the Contract Compliance Analyst flags the expiry milestone from the obligation calendar, the Procurement Category Manager decides whether to renew or sunset the relationship and, if renewing, negotiates updated pricing, rebate, and SLA terms with the supplier, Procurement Legal Counsel drafts the amendment, and the Contract Manager chases supplier pricing confirmation through a bounded escalation window before routing the amendment for delegated approval, executing counter-signatures, and having the Category Manager publish the renewed contract to the sourcing catalog so requisitioners can transact against it.

sipoc.process: scan the obligation calendar for the contract's stated end date and auto-renewal notice window, generating an alert at 120/90/60-day milestones ahead of expiry
benchmark current pricing against market indices and negotiate updated rebate tiers, volume commitments, and SLA terms for the renewal term
determine whether the category requires a competitive re-bid routed to Supplier Selection or a planned sunset with transition-out sourcing
prepare the contract amendment or restated agreement incorporating updated commercial terms and any revised risk clauses
submit the amendment through the delegation-of-authority approval workflow per the updated contract value tier
route the amendment for internal counter-signature and supplier execution via the e-signature platform, validating both signatures recorded
issue a reminder to the supplier's commercial contact citing the renewal deadline and request a signed pricing confirmation annex
update the source-to-pay catalog and CLM system with renewed term dates, pricing, and rebate schedule, and reset the obligation monitoring calendar

data.label: Active Renewed Contract Enabled for Requisitioning

requisition-intake.name: Requisition Intake
requisition-intake.description: In a typical CPG procurement organisation the Requisitioner initiates this process the moment a plant, brand, or category team has an active supplier contract or catalog item plus a genuine business purchase need, capturing that need as a draft purchase requisition and populating cost-centre code, unit of measure, and justification narrative against each line before the Procurement Coordinator screens the requisition for mandatory-field completeness, looping a clarification request back to the Requisitioner when cost-centre code, GL mapping, or justification is missing and withdrawing the requisition only after repeated non-response, while a Category Buyer separately cross-references the item against the preferred-catalog and standing-contract register, flagging any off-catalog sourcing need rather than blocking it, so that the Procurement Coordinator can finally assign a value-band routing code and stage the requisition for downstream budget checking.

data.label: Active Catalog Contract & Purchase Need Confirmed

sipoc.input: Active Catalog Contract & Purchase Need Confirmed
sipoc.process: Requisitioner selects the active contract or catalog line item and records quantity, need-by date, and delivery plant against the identified purchase need
Requisitioner adds GL/cost-centre code, unit of measure, unit-price reference, and business justification narrative to each requisition line
Category Buyer matches the requisitioned item description and SKU against the preferred-catalog and standing-contract register for the category
Procurement Coordinator scans mandatory fields including cost centre, GL code, and justification against the intake checklist and emails the requisitioner the specific gaps
Requisitioner reviews the flagged gaps and either resubmits corrected field values or remains unresponsive past the coordinator's follow-up cadence
Category Buyer annotates the requisition as an off-catalog, non-contract purchase requiring buyer-led sourcing attention downstream
Procurement Coordinator assigns the value-band routing code that will determine budget-check and approval-tier sequencing

data.label: Purchase Requisition Ready for Budget Check

budget-policy-check.name: Budget & Policy Check
budget-policy-check.description: Once a routed purchase requisition arrives, the Finance Business Partner in this CPG procurement organisation cross-references the accountable cost centre's remaining OPEX or CAPEX budget line, year-to-date committed spend, and open purchase-order commitments against the requisition value, escalating any overrun to the Cost Centre Manager who must either reallocate budget for a recheck or reject the spend outright, while a Compliance Officer separately cross-references maverick-spend thresholds, preferred-supplier mandates, tiered approval-matrix rules, and segregation-of-duties requirements, looping a policy exception query back to the Requisitioner for a missing cost-centre code or justification and withdrawing the requisition only after repeated non-response, before finally certifying a policy-compliant requisition as ready for approval routing.

data.label: Purchase Requisition Ready for Budget Check

sipoc.input: Purchase Requisition Ready for Budget Check
sipoc.process: Finance Business Partner interrogates the cost centre's remaining OPEX/CAPEX budget line, YTD committed spend, and open PO commitments against the requisition value
Compliance Officer interrogates maverick-spend thresholds, preferred-supplier mandate, tiered approval-matrix rules, and segregation-of-duties checks against the requisition
Finance Business Partner packages the shortfall amount and remaining budget-line detail into an overrun notice routed to the accountable cost-centre owner
Compliance Officer records the passed policy checks and affixes a compliance certification stamp to the requisition record
Compliance Officer identifies the missing cost-centre code or justification narrative required to close the policy exception and routes a query to the requisitioner

data.label: Policy-Certified Requisition Ready for Approval Routing

sipoc.process: Requisitioner supplies the outstanding cost-centre code or written business justification requested by compliance

requisition-approval.name: Requisition Approval
requisition-approval.description: With a policy-certified requisition routed for sign-off, the Procurement Coordinator in this CPG procurement organisation maps its value band against the delegation-of-authority matrix to determine whether cost-centre-manager or procurement-approver sign-off applies, the Cost Centre Manager then checks for a required vendor quote or business-case attachment, looping a documentation request back through the coordinator to the requisitioner and withdrawing the requisition only after repeated non-response, before either endorsing lower-value requisitions directly against their own delegated limit or passing higher-value requisitions to the Procurement Approver, who adjudicates category-strategy alignment and prior open commitments and either rejects the spend outright or approves it, so the coordinator can compile a single final approval record staged for PO conversion.

data.label: Policy-Certified Requisition Ready for Approval Routing

sipoc.input: Policy-Certified Requisition Ready for Approval Routing
sipoc.process: Procurement Coordinator maps the requisition value band against the delegation-of-authority matrix to determine whether cost-centre-manager or procurement-approver sign-off applies
Cost Centre Manager identifies the missing vendor quote or business-case attachment required for sign-off and routes the gap back through the coordinator
Cost Centre Manager signs off against their own delegated-authority limit and confirms cost-centre ownership of the spend
Procurement Coordinator obtains the outstanding quote or business-case attachment from the requisitioner and reattaches it to the requisition record
Procurement Approver interrogates the value band against the delegation-of-authority matrix, category strategy alignment, and prior open commitments
Procurement Coordinator consolidates the approval signature and timestamp from whichever tier approved into a single approval record staged for PO conversion
Procurement Approver documents the budget, category, or policy misalignment rationale and closes the requisition record as declined

data.label: Approved Requisition Ready for PO Conversion

pr-to-po-conversion.name: PR-to-PO Conversion
pr-to-po-conversion.description: Taking the approved requisition handed off from Requisition Approval, the Procurement Buyer in this CPG procurement organisation transcribes line items, quantities, delivery dates, and cost-centre codes into a draft purchase order, looping a GL/cost-element mapping query back through the Procurement Coordinator to the requisitioner when the coding is invalid and withdrawing the conversion only after repeated non-response, while the Category Manager cross-references the category contract register to assign a preferred-supplier code and negotiated pricing, dispatching a spot-quote request when no standing contract pricing exists and redirecting the requisition to a formal sourcing event when the spot-quote value exceeds the delegated tactical-sourcing threshold, so the buyer can finally consolidate vendor, pricing, and line detail into a purchase order draft staged for formal PO creation and dispatch.

data.label: Approved Requisition Ready for PO Conversion

sipoc.input: Approved Requisition Ready for PO Conversion
sipoc.process: Procurement Buyer transcribes approved requisition line items, quantities, delivery dates, and cost-centre codes into the PO header and line template within the purchasing module
Category Manager cross-references the category contract register for the item to identify the mandated preferred-supplier code and negotiated contract terms
Procurement Buyer identifies the unmapped or invalid GL/cost-element code against the chart of accounts and routes a query back to the requisition originator via the coordinator
Procurement Coordinator retrieves the correct GL/cost-element code from the requisitioner and updates the PO line coding accordingly
Category Manager identifies the absence of standing contract pricing and drafts an off-contract spot-quote request within category sourcing guidelines

data.label: PO Conversion Withdrawn — GL Mapping Unresolved

sipoc.process: Procurement Buyer obtains and records the vendor spot-quote pricing and delivery terms within the delegated tactical-sourcing spend threshold

data.label: PO Conversion Halted — Formal Sourcing Event Required

sipoc.process: Procurement Buyer consolidates the vendor code, contract or spot-quote pricing, and line-item detail into a finalised PO draft record staged for formal PO number assignment

data.label: PO Draft Ready for Formal Creation

po-creation-dispatch.name: PO Creation & Dispatch
po-creation-dispatch.description: In CPG procurement, once a requisition has cleared budget and category approvals it is converted by the Purchasing Specialist into a formal purchase order, pulling negotiated contract pricing, payment terms, and Incoterms into the PO template before the Procurement Buyer checks whether the order value trips the delegation-of-authority threshold requiring Procurement Manager sign-off; once authorized, routing and transmission-channel details are finalized against the supplier master record and the Supplier Account Manager dispatches the PO through EDI, portal, or email, monitoring for a transmission receipt signal and falling back to an alternate channel with a bounded retry count before escalating unconfirmed dispatches to the Procurement Manager.

data.label: Approved Requisition Converted to PO Draft

sipoc.input: Approved Requisition Converted to PO Draft
sipoc.process: Purchasing Specialist maps requisition line items into the PO template, pulling negotiated unit price, payment terms, and Incoterms from the master supplier contract and entering delivery quantities against each line
Procurement Manager reviews total PO commitment value, remaining budget line availability, and delegation-of-authority limits before authorizing the draft in the approval workflow

Purchasing Specialist confirms the ship-to location, carrier routing instructions, and preferred transmission channel (EDI, supplier portal, or email) against the supplier master record before release
Supplier Account Manager releases the PO through the designated transmission channel and opens the supplier portal or EDI acknowledgment queue to monitor for a delivery receipt signal

data.label: Reissue Purchase Order via Fallback Channel

sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager resends the purchase order through an alternate channel such as direct email or fax to the supplier contact and logs the resend attempt against the transmission retry policy

change-cancellation.name: Change / Cancellation
change-cancellation.description: After a purchase order has been dispatched to a CPG supplier, front-line teams frequently must revisit it before goods ship: the Procurement Buyer fields change or cancellation requests, the Purchasing Specialist logs the specific field-level modification or cancellation reason, and full cancellations are executed by the Procurement Buyer and confirmed with the supplier while goods-in-transit disputes are escalated to the Procurement Manager; amendments to quantity, price, date, or ship-to are instead redrafted by the Purchasing Specialist, cleared through Procurement Manager approval if they exceed the threshold, retransmitted by the Supplier Account Manager, and chased for acknowledgment with a bounded follow-up cycle before an unacknowledged revision is escalated.

sipoc.process: Purchasing Specialist captures the requested modification—quantity revision, delivery date shift, unit price correction, or full cancellation—against the PO number and reason code in the change log

data.label: Finalized Purchase Order Awaiting Supplier Confirmation

sipoc.process: Procurement Buyer verifies no goods have shipped or been received against the PO, then executes the cancellation transaction and notifies the supplier and finance of the closed commitment
Purchasing Specialist updates the affected PO fields—quantity, unit price, delivery date, or ship-to address—in the purchasing system and increments the PO revision number
Procurement Manager engages the supplier account team to resolve the disputed cancellation, assessing goods-in-transit status, restocking fees, and cancellation cost liability
Procurement Manager reviews the revised commitment value against remaining budget and delegation-of-authority limits before authorizing the amendment

data.label: Purchase Order Cancellation Escalated — Dispute Unresolved

sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager reissues the revised PO to the supplier through the original transmission channel, flagging it as a numbered revision of the prior purchase order

data.label: Finalized Purchase Order Awaiting Supplier Confirmation

sipoc.input: Amended Purchase Order Dispatched [Receipt Not Acknowledged]
sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager resends the amended PO through an alternate channel and logs the resend attempt against the acknowledgment tracking record

data.label: Purchase Order Amendment Escalated — No Supplier Acknowledgment

order-confirmation.name: Order Confirmation
order-confirmation.description: With a finalized purchase order in hand, the Supplier Account Manager requests formal order acknowledgment from the CPG supplier and chases a non-responsive supplier through a bounded follow-up cycle before escalating a stalled acknowledgment to the Procurement Manager; once acknowledgment arrives, the Purchasing Specialist validates the supplier-confirmed price, quantity, and delivery date against the original PO, and the Procurement Buyer either lets the Supplier Account Manager lock in the committed delivery date or raises a term discrepancy notice that the supplier must correct, with unresolved mismatches escalated to the Procurement Manager for supplier-leadership engagement.

data.label: Finalized Purchase Order Awaiting Supplier Confirmation

sipoc.input: Finalized Purchase Order Awaiting Supplier Confirmation
sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager transmits an acknowledgment request to the supplier via EDI 855, portal, or email, specifying the PO number, line items, unit prices, and requested delivery date requiring confirmation
Purchasing Specialist cross-checks the supplier's acknowledged unit price, quantity, and delivery date against the original purchase order line items to flag any discrepancy
Supplier Account Manager re-contacts the supplier's order desk, restates the outstanding PO number, and logs the follow-up date against the acknowledgment SLA timer

data.label: Purchase Order Escalated — No Supplier Acknowledgment

sipoc.process: Procurement Buyer issues a term discrepancy notice to the supplier account team, citing the specific deviating price, quantity, or delivery date field requiring correction

data.label: Supplier Corrects Terms to Match PO?

sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager records the supplier's committed delivery date and shipping method into the order confirmation record and notifies the buyer that the delivery schedule is locked
sipoc.output: Order Confirmation Record — Delivery Date Committed

sipoc.process: Procurement Manager engages supplier leadership to resolve the persistent term mismatch, weighing re-sourcing options against conditional acceptance of the supplier's terms

data.label: Order Confirmation Record — Delivery Date Committed
Purchase Order Escalated — Unresolved Term Dispute

expediting-follow-up.name: Expediting & Follow-up
expediting-follow-up.description: Between order confirmation and receiving dock activity, the Supplier Account Manager tracks the shipment against its committed delivery date and, when the schedule looks at risk, the Purchasing Specialist issues a formal expedite request to the supplier; a supplier who declines a recovery date triggers Procurement Manager escalation and an alternate-sourcing decision, while a confirmed or recovered schedule moves into advance-ship-notice monitoring, where a bounded follow-up cycle chases a missing ASN before escalating, and a received ASN is validated by the Purchasing Specialist against the receiving dock schedule to confirm the purchase order is ready for goods receipt posting.

data.label: Order Confirmation Record — Delivery Date Committed

sipoc.input: Order Confirmation Record — Delivery Date Committed
sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager compares the shipment status feed against the committed delivery date using the supplier's tracking portal or the EDI 856 advance ship notice trigger window

data.label: Delivery Date at Risk of Slippage?

sipoc.process: Purchasing Specialist sends a formal expedite request to the supplier specifying the required recovery date, referencing the PO number and the shortfall against the committed schedule

Supplier Account Manager watches the EDI 856 advance ship notice feed for carrier assignment, tracking number, and actual ship date confirming the shipment is moving against the committed or recovered delivery date
Procurement Manager reviews the supplier's delivery performance history and expedite refusal, weighing options including split shipment, alternate carrier, or emergency re-sourcing

sipoc.input: Advance Ship Notice Monitoring Log [Notice Received]
sipoc.process: Purchasing Specialist verifies the advance ship notice carrier, tracking number, and expected arrival date align with the receiving dock schedule and confirms the PO is ready for goods receipt
sipoc.output: Purchase Order Ready for Goods Receipt Posting

data.label: Purchase Order Escalated — Alternate Recovery Approved
Follow Up for Missing Ship Notice

sipoc.input: Advance Ship Notice Monitoring Log [Notice Not Received]
sipoc.process: Supplier Account Manager contacts the supplier's logistics desk to request the outstanding advance ship notice and logs the follow-up attempt against the ASN SLA timer

data.label: Purchase Order Escalated — Delivery Risk Unresolved
Purchase Order Ready for Goods Receipt Posting
Purchase Order Escalated — No Advance Ship Notice

goods-receipt-posting.name: Goods Receipt Posting
goods-receipt-posting.description: Goods Receipt Posting picks up the moment a carrier pulls up to the plant or DC dock against a purchase order already confirmed through Expediting & Follow-up; the Dock Supervisor assigns the door and unloading sequence, the Warehouse Receiving Clerk offloads pallets onto the staging lane and screens the packing slip for a valid PO reference, looping a buyer follow-up and wait cycle with an escalation threshold whenever that reference is missing, then counts received cases line by line against the PO before the Inventory Control Lead reconciles quantity to tolerance, logging a variance exception to the Buyer / Procurement Coordinator whenever cases run short or over, and finally posts the goods receipt in the ERP so the resulting Posted Goods Receipt Note can open the Service Entry Sheet workflow for any service lines riding on the same purchase order.

sipoc.process: Cross-checks the carrier manifest number and appointment slot against the open PO delivery schedule board, assigns a dock door and sets the unloading sequence
Directs the forklift crew to offload pallets from the trailer and stacks cartons by PO line onto the staging lane

sipoc.input: Unloaded Pallets Staged At Dock [PO Reference Confirmed]
sipoc.process: Scans carton barcodes and tallies received case quantity per PO line against the packing slip unit count, rounding to nearest full case pack

sipoc.input: Unloaded Pallets Staged At Dock [No PO Reference]
sipoc.process: Emails the buyer distribution mailbox with the carrier BOL number, vendor name and case count, flags the shipment as held pending PO identification
Holds the flagged pallets in the quarantine staging bay and polls the buyer response queue every two hours for the matching PO number
Documents the case-level shortage or overage against the PO line, tags the variance percentage and attaches dock photo evidence for buyer visibility
Posts the goods receipt document in the ERP against the open PO line, recording confirmed quantity, batch/lot number and dock timestamp

data.label: Receipt Held — No PO Reference Confirmed

service-entry-sheet.name: Service Entry Sheet
service-entry-sheet.description: Service Entry Sheet takes over once the Posted Goods Receipt Note confirms a mixed purchase order's equipment or material lines have landed, and the Plant Operations Supervisor walks the job site with the vendor crew lead to confirm statement-of-work tasks are complete before the Service Owner / Requisitioner drafts labor hours and unit quantities into the SES form; the Plant Operations Supervisor checks for the requisitioner's sign-off, looping a reminder-and-wait cycle with an escalation threshold when that confirmation is slow to arrive, after which the Procurement Contract Analyst validates the entered rates against the master service agreement and the AP Service Entry Auditor either bounces mismatched rates back for correction or posts the finalized Service Entry Sheet so it can flow into Quality Inspection alongside the physical receipt.

sipoc.process: Walks the job site with the vendor crew lead and checks off statement-of-work tasks against the service PO scope line by line
Enters labor hours, unit-of-measure quantities and service descriptions into the SES form referencing the service PO line and cost center
Cross-checks the entered labor rate and unit price per service line against the master service agreement rate card and PO unit price

sipoc.input: Draft Service Entry Sheet [No Owner Confirmation]
sipoc.process: Sends the draft SES back to the requisitioner's approval queue with a reminder flagging the missing sign-off line
Monitors the SES approval queue daily and pings the requisitioner through the ticketing system if the request is unopened after 24 hours

sipoc.input: Rate-Validated Service Entry Sheet [Rate Within Ceiling]
sipoc.process: Posts the finalized service entry sheet in the ERP against the service PO line, generating the SES document number for downstream three-way match

data.label: Return Entry Sheet For Rate Correction

sipoc.input: Rate-Validated Service Entry Sheet [Rate Exceeds Ceiling]
sipoc.process: Flags the mismatched line item, annotates the contracted rate versus entered rate delta and routes the sheet back to the requisitioner for correction

data.label: Service Entry Held — No Owner Confirmation

quality-inspection.name: Quality Inspection
quality-inspection.description: Quality Inspection begins when the Posted Service Entry Sheet clears the associated lot for quality review, and the Warehouse Receiving Clerk moves the pallets into the quarantine hold cage before the Quality Inspector pulls a sample per the AQL sampling plan and the QA Lab Technician runs the sampled units against the item's weight, fill, seal and microbial specifications; once the technician submits the result sheet into the quality queue, the inspector loops a lab follow-up and wait cycle with an escalation threshold if the sheet never lands, then adjudicates the results against spec tolerance and hands the disposition to the Inventory Control Lead, who either releases a conforming lot straight to put-away or segregates a failing lot into the non-conforming cage so the inspector can log the formal Non-Conformance Report that opens Returns & Rejections.

sipoc.process: Moves the received pallets from the dock staging lane into the quarantine hold cage, applies a quality-hold license plate label and updates the WMS status to hold
Selects sample units per the ANSI/ASQ Z1.4 acceptable quality level sampling plan matched to the lot size and the item's inspection severity level
Runs the sampled units through the item's quality specification tests — net weight, fill level, seal integrity and microbial swab where applicable — logging measured values against the spec limits

data.label: Submit Result Sheet To Quality Queue

sipoc.process: Uploads the completed result sheet with measured values into the quality management system's inspection queue for inspector sign-off
Compares each measured attribute in the result sheet to the approved specification tolerance band and applies the lot disposition rule from the quality manual
Calls the QA lab bench, confirms sample receipt and testing status, and flags the lot as SLA-at-risk in the quality hold log
Checks the quality queue every four hours for the resubmitted result sheet and records elapsed wait time against the SLA clock
Physically moves the failed lot to the non-conforming materials cage and applies a red reject tag noting lot number, defect code and inspector ID

data.label: Quality Hold Escalated — No Lab Result Received

sipoc.process: Files the formal non-conformance report in the quality system, citing the failed attribute, sample size, defect rate and photographic evidence

returns-rejections.name: Returns & Rejections
returns-rejections.description: Returns & Rejections opens on the Non-Conformance Report handed off from Quality Inspection, with the Inventory Control Lead weighing the defect pattern and lot value against the disposition matrix to decide between full rejection and a partial accept-with-deviation; on a partial call the Warehouse Receiving Clerk segregates the conforming units for put-away and the Inventory Control Lead re-posts the goods receipt at the confirmed conforming quantity, while the Vendor Returns Coordinator opens a return merchandise authorization for the rejected remainder, looping an account-manager escalation and wait cycle with a threshold decision whenever the vendor is slow to confirm the RMA, before the Warehouse Receiving Clerk stages the rejected units for carrier pickup and the Buyer / Procurement Coordinator books a vendor debit memo, so the Inventory Control Lead can close a fully-rejected lot with no invoice match required or, on the partial path, finalize a Posted Accepted Goods Receipt that is ready for the three-way match against the supplier invoice.

sipoc.process: Reviews the defect rate, sample failure pattern and lot value against the quality manual's disposition matrix to decide between full rejection, partial accept-with-deviation, or return-to-vendor for rework
Physically separates the passing units from the rejected units in the quality hold cage and re-labels the conforming sub-lot with a new putaway license plate

data.label: Adjust Goods Receipt For Conforming Units

sipoc.process: Deducts the rejected unit count from the originally posted receipt quantity and re-posts the goods receipt document to reflect only the conforming units accepted into inventory

Opens a return merchandise authorization case in the vendor's returns portal citing lot number, defect code and rejected unit quantity, and requests an RMA number with pickup or destroy-in-place instruction
Palletizes the rejected units with the RMA number and vendor return label, schedules an outbound carrier pickup and captures a proof-of-pickup signature
Escalates the open RMA request to the vendor's account manager through the supplier relationship email alias, citing the aging return case number
Issues a debit memo to the vendor for the returned unit quantity and value, referencing the RMA number and original PO line for accounts payable visibility
Monitors the vendor portal daily for RMA number issuance and logs elapsed days since the original return request

data.label: Finalize Accepted Receipt For Invoice Match

sipoc.process: Confirms the vendor debit memo value reconciles with the adjusted goods receipt quantity, then finalizes the posted goods receipt status as ready for three-way match
sipoc.output: Posted Accepted Goods Receipt Ready For Invoice Match

data.label: PO Line Closed — Full Lot Returned, No Invoice Match Required
Return Held — No Vendor RMA Confirmation
Posted Accepted Goods Receipt Ready For Invoice Match

invoice-capture-coding.name: Invoice Capture & Coding
invoice-capture-coding.description: In a CPG accounts-payable operation, once Returns & Rejections has cleared a receipted goods invoice for processing, the AP Invoice Processor captures the raw supplier invoice image or e-invoice feed, extracting header, line-item, and purchase-order reference data through OCR or manual keying; where the purchase-order reference is absent the processor raises a query back to the requisitioning cost centre and parks the invoice pending a response or escalation, while invoices carrying a valid PO reference are cross-referenced by the AP Coding Analyst against the receipted PO lines and assigned general-ledger, cost-centre, and tax coding per the coding matrix, with incomplete coding triggering a further clarification query before the AP Coding Analyst finalises and releases a fully coded invoice record into the duplicate-screening queue.

sipoc.process: Scan or OCR-extract invoice header fields, line items, PO reference number, and remittance data from the received supplier invoice document
Issue a query to the requisitioning cost centre for the missing purchase-order reference number needed to route the invoice
Hold the invoice in a pending-information queue and track elapsed query age against the response service-level target
Cross-reference the invoice line items, quantities, and unit prices against the corresponding receipted purchase-order lines in the PO record
Apply cost-centre, GL account, and tax-code combinations to each invoice line per the coding matrix rules for the matched PO category

data.label: Invoice Parked — PO Reference Not Provided

sipoc.process: Lock the validated header, line, and coding data into the invoice record and release it from the capture queue for downstream screening
sipoc.output: Coded Invoice Ready for Duplicate Screening

sipoc.process: Raise a coding-clarification request identifying the specific line items lacking a valid cost-centre or GL account assignment

data.label: Coded Invoice Ready for Duplicate Screening

sipoc.process: Hold the invoice in a pending-coding queue and track elapsed query age against the clarification service-level target

duplicate-detection.name: Duplicate Detection
duplicate-detection.description: Building on the coded invoice record released from Invoice Capture & Coding, the AP Invoice Processor generates a matching fingerprint from vendor identifier, invoice number, date, and gross amount and the AP Analyst cross-references it against open and paid invoice history in the AP ledger; invoices with no history match pass straight through to the routing queue, while a flagged potential match is investigated by the AP Analyst against the matched record's line detail and supporting documentation, requesting original invoice or receipt documentation from records archive when evidence is insufficient to confirm the match; a confirmed true duplicate is blocked and rejected by the AP Team Lead to prevent double payment, whereas a false-positive match is cleared and the invoice released for PO/non-PO routing determination.

data.label: Coded Invoice Ready for Duplicate Screening

sipoc.input: Coded Invoice Ready for Duplicate Screening
sipoc.process: Compute a matching fingerprint from supplier ID, invoice number, invoice date, and gross amount fields for automated comparison against invoice history
Cross-reference the fingerprint against open and paid invoice records in the AP ledger for exact and near-exact matches on vendor, number, date, and amount
Compare the flagged invoice against the matched prior record's line detail, PO reference, and supporting documentation to distinguish a genuine resubmission or credit correction from a true duplicate
Request the original invoice image, supporting receipt, or prior remittance advice from records archive to distinguish the flagged match from the invoice under review
Mark the invoice record as blocked-duplicate in the AP ledger and log the original matched invoice reference to prevent payment
Hold the invoice in a pending-investigation queue and track elapsed request age against the documentation service-level target
Annotate the invoice record with the false-positive resolution rationale and release the hold placed during investigation
Move the screened invoice from the duplicate-check queue into the routing queue for PO and non-PO determination

data.label: Invoice Parked — Duplicate Documentation Not Provided

non-po-routing.name: Non-PO Routing
non-po-routing.description: Receiving the duplicate-cleared invoice, the AP Invoice Processor classifies each invoice against its purchase-order status, forwarding PO-backed invoices directly into the approval queue since PO-line coding already exists; invoices identified as non-PO spend are handed to the AP Analyst, who interrogates line descriptions, vendor category, and prior transaction history to identify the accountable cost-centre and budget owner, raising an enquiry to the relevant business partner and escalating through the AP Team Lead to a parked status if the owner cannot be confirmed within threshold; once the cost centre is confirmed, the AP Analyst applies the non-PO GL coding matrix to assign account, tax, and expense-type codes, and the Cost Centre Approver determines the required approval tier against the delegation-of-authority matrix before queuing the invoice for approval routing.

sipoc.process: Interrogate the invoice header for a valid open purchase-order number and confirm whether the invoice qualifies as PO-backed or non-PO spend requiring special routing

data.label: Route PO Invoice to Approval Queue

sipoc.process: Forward the PO-backed invoice directly into the approval-workflow queue since PO line coding already exists and non-PO cost-centre assignment is not required
sipoc.output: PO-Backed Invoice Queued for Approval Routing

sipoc.process: Interrogate invoice line descriptions, vendor spend category, and prior transaction history to identify the cost-centre and budget owner accountable for the non-PO spend

data.label: PO-Backed Invoice Queued for Approval Routing

sipoc.process: Apply the non-PO GL coding matrix rules for the identified cost centre and spend category to assign account, tax, and expense-type codes to the invoice
Issue an enquiry to the vendor-category business partner requesting confirmation of the accountable cost centre for the unidentified non-PO spend
Evaluate the coded invoice amount against the delegation-of-authority matrix to determine the required approval tier and named approver route
Hold the invoice in a pending-assignment queue and track elapsed enquiry age against the confirmation service-level target

data.label: Invoice Parked — Cost Centre Not Identified

approval-workflow.name: Approval Workflow
approval-workflow.description: Taking the invoice queued for approval routing from Non-PO Routing, the AP Analyst dispatches it to the named Cost Centre Approver identified by the delegation-of-authority tier, reassigning to a nominated delegate and escalating through the AP Team Lead to a parked status if the named approver remains unavailable beyond threshold; the Cost Centre Approver inspects the coded line detail, amount, and budget availability against personal knowledge of the goods or services received, returning disputed coding to the AP Analyst for correction and resubmission where inaccurate; accurate invoices within the approver's delegated authorisation limit are signed off directly, while invoices exceeding that limit are escalated to the Finance Manager for secondary high-value authorisation, producing an approved or dual-authorised invoice ready for 2/3-way matching.

sipoc.process: Route the coded invoice to the named approver identified by the delegation-of-authority tier via the approval notification queue
Identify the nominated delegate approver from the authorisation matrix and reroute the pending invoice notification to the delegate
Hold the invoice in the pending-authorisation queue and track elapsed delegate response time against the approval service-level target
Interrogate the invoice line descriptions, GL coding, and amount against the approver's own knowledge of the goods or services received and budget availability
Annotate the specific coding discrepancy identified during inspection and return the invoice to the AP coding queue for correction before resubmission
Apply electronic sign-off confirming the coding, amount, and business justification are accurate and within the approver's delegated authorisation limit
Amend the disputed GL account, cost-centre, or tax-code assignment per the approver's annotation and resubmit the invoice to the approval queue
Forward the invoice with supporting business justification to the Finance Manager for secondary authorisation as required above the approver's delegated limit
Interrogate the invoice value, business justification, and budget impact against the high-value spend policy before granting secondary sign-off

2-3-way-matching.name: 2/3-Way Matching
2-3-way-matching.description: In the CPG accounts payable operation, once an invoice has been coded and approved through the approval workflow, the AP Matching Specialist classifies each invoice line by PO type to determine whether a two-way match (PO to invoice, typical for non-stock services, freight, or marketing spend) or a three-way match (PO to goods receipt to invoice, typical for raw material, packaging, and finished-goods purchases) applies; for three-way lines the GR Coordinator retrieves the posted goods receipt from the warehouse receiving system, and where receipt has not yet been posted the AP Matching Specialist dispatches a receipt-status inquiry so the invoice is held in a pending queue until the GR posts or an aging threshold forces the line to be blocked and referred to the AP Team Lead; once GR data is available the AP Matching Specialist compares PO price and quantity, invoice price and quantity, and GR received quantity line by line, while two-way lines are compared directly on PO versus invoice price and quantity, and the AP Team Lead queues the resulting match outcome for the downstream tolerance and variance assessment step.

sipoc.input: Coded, Approved Invoice with PO Reference
sipoc.process: Interrogate PO type (goods vs. services), line count, and receipt-required flag on the approved invoice to classify it as a 2-way or 3-way match
Query the warehouse receiving system for the posted goods receipt document, received quantity, and receipt date against the PO line
Compare PO unit price and quantity against invoice price and quantity for non-receipted PO lines such as services or non-stock items
Compare PO price and quantity, invoice price and quantity, and GR received quantity line-by-line for exact agreement
Send a receipt-status inquiry to the GR Coordinator referencing the PO number and expected delivery window
Log the match outcome and any variance flags into the tolerance-assessment work queue with match type and dollar variance tagged
sipoc.output: Match Result Queued for Tolerance Assessment

sipoc.process: Hold the invoice in the pending-receipt queue while monitoring the dock receiving log for completion of the PO line

data.label: Match Result Queued for Tolerance Assessment

tolerance-variance.name: Tolerance & Variance
tolerance-variance.description: Once the AP Matching Specialist's match result is queued, the AP Tolerance Analyst calculates the percentage price variance between PO and invoice unit price and the percentage quantity variance between invoiced and received quantity for each CPG purchase line — raw material, packaging, co-manufacturing, or trade-spend — and tests both against the tolerance policy matrix, typically plus-or-minus 2% on price and 5% on quantity; lines within threshold are auto-classified as immaterial and queued directly, while lines exceeding threshold are categorized by root cause as a price change, quantity discrepancy, unit-of-measure mismatch, or freight/tax difference, with price-change variances routed to the Procurement Buyer for clarification against the contract or price-amendment history, held in a pending-clarification queue and escalated by the AP Team Lead if aging thresholds are breached, and non-price variances routed straight to the exception queue, with the AP Team Lead compiling every disposition into a single classified tolerance assessment record for exception resolution.

data.label: Match Result Queued for Tolerance Assessment

sipoc.input: Match Result Queued for Tolerance Assessment
sipoc.process: Calculate percentage price variance between PO unit price and invoice unit price, and percentage quantity variance between invoiced quantity and matched receipt or PO quantity, for each match line
Flag the line as immaterial per the tolerance policy matrix (price plus-or-minus 2%, quantity plus-or-minus 5%) and stamp an auto-clear code
Categorize the variance driver as price change, quantity discrepancy, unit-of-measure mismatch, or freight/tax difference
Send a price-variance clarification request to the Procurement Buyer citing PO price, invoice price, and contract reference
Forward the quantity, unit-of-measure, or tax/freight variance record directly to the exception queue without buyer clarification
Hold the variance in the pending-clarification queue while checking contract or price-amendment history for the disputed line
Incorporate the buyer's price-change rationale into the variance record and set disposition as approved-price-change or confirmed-billing-error
Consolidate the variance classification, disposition, and dollar exposure into a single tolerance assessment record for exception-queue intake

exception-resolution.name: Exception Resolution
exception-resolution.description: Working from the classified tolerance assessment, the AP Exceptions Analyst sorts each CPG invoice exception into a category — quantity discrepancy, price dispute, or unit-of-measure/tax/freight difference — and resolves it case by case: quantity discrepancies trigger a goods-receipt correction request to the GR Coordinator, who recounts and re-posts the warehouse receipt, with the exception held open and escalated by the AP Team Lead if the correction ages past threshold; price disputes trigger a supplier credit-memo or invoice-correction request routed through the Procurement Buyer, who confirms the credit reconciles the billed price to the contracted price; and unit-of-measure, tax, or freight discrepancies are recalculated directly by the AP Exceptions Analyst; the AP Team Lead then closes out each resolved exception record and releases the invoice line for GR/IR reconciliation.

sipoc.process: Sort the variance record into an exception category: price dispute, quantity discrepancy, unit-of-measure mismatch, or freight/tax difference
Send a request to the GR Coordinator to confirm the actual received quantity and re-post the goods receipt if short- or over-received
Hold the exception in the pending-correction queue while the warehouse recounts and re-posts the goods receipt document
Initiate a credit-memo or invoice-correction request to the supplier via the Procurement Buyer, citing agreed contract price versus billed price
Recalculate the invoice using the correct unit-of-measure conversion or tax/freight allocation and determine the adjusted variance amount
Verify the supplier-issued credit memo or corrected invoice reconciles the price differential to the contracted price
Re-match the invoiced quantity against the corrected GR quantity and confirm the variance is eliminated within tolerance
Confirm each exception's variance is eliminated or formally accepted, log the resolution outcome, and release the invoice line for GR/IR reconciliation

gr-ir-clearing.name: GR/IR Clearing
gr-ir-clearing.description: With the exception resolution record closed, the AP Clearing Specialist retrieves the GR/IR suspense account balance for the PO line — the accrued goods-receipt value against the accumulated invoice-receipt value — and computes the residual open balance; a zero residual triggers a full clearing journal entry posted by the GL Accountant, a small residual within the delegated write-off threshold triggers an immaterial write-off entry, and a material residual is investigated by the GR Coordinator against the warehouse receiving log and in-transit shipment schedule for partial shipments or unposted receipts, with an outstanding receipt held open on the GR/IR aging report and escalated by the AP Team Lead if the gap ages past threshold, or referred as a material discrepancy for reconciliation review if no further receipt is expected; once the clearing entry posts, the AP Clearing Specialist confirms it in the AP subledger and stamps the PO line as matched, cleared, and ready for payment.

sipoc.process: Query the GR/IR suspense account for the PO line to extract the accrued goods-receipt value and accumulated invoice-receipt value
Subtract the accumulated invoice value from the accrued goods-receipt value to derive the residual open balance for the PO line
Post the journal entry debiting GR/IR suspense and crediting accounts payable to fully clear the PO line balance
Post a small-dollar write-off journal entry per delegation-of-authority policy to close the residual GR/IR balance below the materiality threshold
Check the warehouse receiving log and in-transit shipment schedule for partial shipments or unposted receipts explaining the residual
Confirm the clearing journal entry posted and stamp the PO line as matched, cleared, and payment-ready in the AP subledger
sipoc.output: Matched, Cleared Invoice Ready for Payment

data.label: Matched, Cleared Invoice Ready for Payment

sipoc.input: Receipt Timing Investigation Result [Additional Receipt Outstanding]
sipoc.process: Send a follow-up notice to the GR Coordinator referencing the outstanding PO quantity and expected delivery date

sipoc.input: Receipt Timing Investigation Result [No Additional Receipt]
sipoc.process: Refer the unresolved material GR/IR variance to Procurement and the AP Team Lead for an account reconciliation review outside the standard clearing cycle
Hold the PO line open on the GR/IR aging report while monitoring the dock receiving log for the remaining shipment quantity

data.label: GR/IR Item Escalated — Aged Open Receipt

payment-proposal.name: Payment Proposal
payment-proposal.description: In CPG accounts payable, Payment Proposal is the SAP F110-style proposal run that converts the pool of matched, GR/IR-cleared invoices into a structured, cash-aware payment due list: the AP Payments Specialist selects open items maturing inside the 14-day due-date window per payment-terms code, holds items with incomplete vendor bank data for correction, and passes the completed list to the Treasury Analyst, who screens it against the rolling cash forecast and the $2M minimum operating cash-position threshold, escalating shortfalls for prioritisation or short-term funding; once cash-cleared, the AP Payments Specialist assembles the items into a Payment Proposal List grouped by payment method and house bank, which the AP Manager authorises directly within the $5M single-run limit or escalates to the Controller for sign-off, releasing an approved proposal list into the downstream discount-optimisation cycle.

data.label: Matched Cleared Invoice Ready for Payment

sipoc.input: Matched Cleared Invoice Ready for Payment
sipoc.process: Filters open vendor line items maturing within the next 14-day due-date window per payment-terms code, excluding items flagged with a payment-block indicator, against the pool of matched, GR/IR-cleared invoices
Compares the aggregate value of the completed due list against the rolling 14-day cash forecast and the $2M minimum operating cash-position threshold held by Treasury

Flags line items with missing or invalid remittance bank details on the vendor master and routes a correction request to the vendor master data owner, holding the item outside the current proposal cycle
Monitors the correction queue for the resubmitted vendor bank record ahead of the next scheduled proposal-run cut-off date
Compiles the cash-cleared due items into a structured proposal list grouped by payment method (ACH, wire, check) and house bank account
Notifies the AP Manager of the projected shortfall against the due-list value and requests prioritisation guidance or a short-term treasury funding transfer before the next run

data.label: Invoice Deferred — Awaiting Vendor Master Correction

sipoc.process: Tracks the incoming short-term funding transfer or intercompany cash sweep against the shortfall amount ahead of the next proposal-run cut-off
Routes proposal batches exceeding the $5M single-run authorisation limit to the Controller for secondary sign-off before release to discount optimisation
sipoc.output: Payment Proposal List Approved for Discount Optimisation

data.label: Payment Proposal List Approved for Discount Optimisation

discount-capture.name: Discount Capture
discount-capture.description: Discount Capture is the CPG treasury discipline of converting the approved Payment Proposal List into a discount-optimised batch: the AP Payments Specialist screens each line for an unexpired early-payment terms code such as 2/10 Net 30, routing lapsed items straight to standard due-date payment, while the Treasury Analyst ranks the still-open discount opportunities by their annualised effective yield against current short-term investment returns and checks that cash or revolving-credit capacity exists to fund early settlement, escalating and awaiting a funding transfer when it does not; once funding is confirmed, the AP Payments Specialist selects the highest-yield opportunities within capacity, merges them with the routed standard-terms items into a single Discount-Optimised Payment Batch, and the AP Manager reconciles its value to the original proposal list before releasing it, escalating any variance beyond the $10,000 tolerance to the Controller.

data.label: Payment Proposal List Approved for Discount Optimisation

sipoc.input: Payment Proposal List Approved for Discount Optimisation
sipoc.process: Scans each proposal line for a cash-discount payment-terms code (e.g., 2/10 Net 30, 1/15 Net 45) and flags items still inside the discount window as of the run date

Calculates the annualised effective return of capturing each cash discount (e.g., a 2%/10-day term equates to roughly 36% annualised) against the current short-term investment yield to rank capture priority

data.label: Route Item to Standard Due-Date Payment
