sipoc.process: Removes lapsed-discount items from the optimisation pool and returns them to the standard net-due-date payment queue for the current run

data.label: Cash Available to Fund Early Payment?

sipoc.process: Selects the highest-yield discount opportunities within confirmed funding capacity and marks them for expedited payment ahead of the standard due date
Requests a short-term intercompany funding transfer or draws on the revolving credit facility to cover the top-ranked discount opportunities ahead of window expiry
Merges expedited discount-capture items with routed standard-terms items into a single batch sequenced by required payment date
Tracks confirmation of the credit-facility draw or funding transfer against the discount-window expiry date for each ranked opportunity

data.label: Batch Value Reconciles to Proposal List?

sipoc.process: Routes batches whose total value deviates from the approved proposal list by more than the $10,000 tolerance to the Controller for variance resolution before release
sipoc.output: Discount-Optimised Payment Batch Released for Payment Run

data.label: Discount-Optimised Payment Batch Released for Payment Run

payment-run-remittance.name: Payment Run & Remittance
payment-run-remittance.description: Payment Run & Remittance is the CPG treasury execution step that turns a released Discount-Optimised Payment Batch into cash leaving the bank: the AP Payments Specialist sequences the batch into ACH, wire, and check sub-runs by vendor payment-method preference, the Treasury Analyst builds and formats the bank transmission file (NACHA, SWIFT MT101) against the house bank account and value date, resolving any bank validation rejection with bank operations ahead of the same-day cut-off before authorising dual-control release of the executed payment run; the AP Payments Specialist then generates and transmits vendor remittance advice mapping paid invoices, discounts taken, and net amounts to each payment reference, escalating any bank-returned or rejected item to the AP Manager for reissue scheduling in the next run.

data.label: Discount-Optimised Payment Batch Released for Payment Run

sipoc.input: Discount-Optimised Payment Batch Released for Payment Run
sipoc.process: Splits the released batch into ACH, wire, and check sub-runs per each vendor's payment-method preference recorded in the vendor master, sequencing each sub-run against its required value date
Formats each sub-run into the bank's required transmission layout (NACHA for ACH, SWIFT MT101 for wire) and applies the house bank account and value date for release
Authorises dual-control release of the validated transmission file to the house bank's payment gateway ahead of the same-day ACH and wire cut-off times

data.label: Dispatch File Correction to Bank Ops

sipoc.process: Routes the rejected transmission file back with the bank's validation error code (e.g., invalid routing number, duplicate trace number) for correction ahead of the cut-off time
Produces line-item remittance advice per vendor, mapping paid invoice numbers, discount taken, and net amount to the corresponding payment reference
Monitors the bank-ops queue for the corrected transmission file ahead of the same-day funds-transfer cut-off time
Distributes remittance advice via EDI 820, email, or vendor portal upload according to each vendor's designated remittance channel
sipoc.output: Executed Payment Run with Remittance Transmitted

data.label: Executed Payment Run with Remittance Transmitted

bank-reconciliation.name: Bank Reconciliation
bank-reconciliation.description: Bank Reconciliation is the terminal CPG P2P control that closes the loop on an executed payment run: the Bank Reconciliation Analyst downloads the daily BAI2/MT940 bank statement and auto-matches its debit lines to the executed payment run by amount, value date, and payment reference, routing any unmatched line to the Treasury Analyst for source-document tracing and escalating unresolved items to bank investigations once the tracing threshold is reached; once fully matched, the Bank Reconciliation Analyst posts fee, FX, and timing-difference adjustment entries and compiles a reconciliation statement with a zero unexplained variance, which the AP Manager signs off as closed within the $500 materiality tolerance — or escalates to the Controller for review beyond it — producing the Closed Payment Reconciliation Record that completes the Procure-to-Pay cycle.

data.label: Executed Payment Run with Remittance Transmitted

sipoc.input: Executed Payment Run with Remittance Transmitted
sipoc.process: Retrieves the prior-day BAI2/MT940 bank statement file for each house bank account tied to the executed payment run's value date
Runs automated matching of statement debit lines against the executed payment run by amount, value date, and payment reference number, applying a $0.01 tolerance

sipoc.input: Matched and Unmatched Line Set [Fully Matched]
sipoc.process: Posts bank fees, FX rounding, and timing-difference adjustment entries identified during matching to the house bank general ledger account

sipoc.input: Matched and Unmatched Line Set [Unmatched Lines Present]
sipoc.process: Routes each unmatched statement line to the Treasury Analyst with the payment reference, amount, and value-date discrepancy for source-document tracing
Assembles the completed reconciliation statement showing book balance, adjusted bank balance, and a zero unexplained variance for the statement period
Traces the disputed statement line against the bank's payment-gateway confirmation log and vendor bank advice pending resolution

data.label: Bank Line Unresolved — Escalated to Bank Investigations

sipoc.process: Routes statements with an unexplained variance above the $500 materiality threshold to the Controller for review and adjustment authorisation before closing
Signs off the statement period as closed, confirming the executed payment run is fully reconciled to the bank statement and the P2P cycle for the batch is complete
sipoc.output: Closed Payment Reconciliation Record — P2P Cycle Complete
